files_2-irec.business_with_brsr_renewable_electricity: Business with BRSR renewable electricity reporting
files_2-irec.calculate_your_electricity_consumption_i:
Calculate your electricity consumption, identify renewable versus non-renewable sources
          and determine the I-REC volume required for your Scope 2 target.
files_2-irec.can_solar_panels_on_our_facility_replace: Can solar panels on our facility replace I-RECs?
files_2-irec.cdp_climate_module_requires_both_locatio:
CDP Climate module requires both location-based and market-based Scope 2 —
            I-RECs are the market-based instrument.
files_2-irec.cdp_requires_companies_disclosing_scope_:
CDP requires companies disclosing Scope 2 to report both location-based and
          market-based emissions. Market-based requires contractual instruments — PPAs, green tariffs or energy
          attribute certificates. I-RECs are the recognised instrument for India.
files_2-irec.companies_that_are_re100_members_or_that:
Companies that are RE100 members or that supply RE100 companies must demonstrate
          100% renewable electricity through verified instruments. I-RECs are accepted by RE100 as a qualifying
          instrument for markets where direct PPAs are not available.
files_2-irec.documentation_package_demonstrating_re10:
Documentation package demonstrating RE100 qualifying instrument retirement for
              RE100 members and their supply chain participants.
files_2-irec.execute_formal_i_rec_retirement_in_the_r:
Execute formal I-REC retirement in the registry, generate retirement certificates and
          verify against your electricity consumption for CDP and GHG Protocol.
files_2-irec.for_energy_buyers_we_design_an_i_rec_pro:
For energy buyers, we design an I-REC procurement strategy aligned to your
                Scope 2 target, CDP reporting requirements and RE100 membership obligations — determining volume,
                vintage, technology mix and country of origin.
files_2-irec.for_renewable_energy_generators_we_manag:
For renewable energy generators, we manage the complete I-REC issuance
                process — generator registration on the I-REC Standard registry, device registration, meter data
                submission and certificate issuance.
files_2-irec.formal_i_rec_retirement_certificates_fro:
Formal I-REC retirement certificates from the I-REC Standard registry — the legal
              documentation of your renewable electricity claims.
files_2-irec.formatted_scope_2_data_for_cdp_climate_q:
Formatted Scope 2 data for CDP Climate questionnaire, distinguishing
              location-based and market-based methods per CDP requirements.
files_2-irec.ghg_protocol_market_based_scope_2_calcul:
GHG Protocol market-based Scope 2 calculation using I-REC retirement data and
              residual emission factors, in CDP-compliant format.
files_2-irec.i_rec_retirement_data_feeds_directly_int:
I-REC retirement data feeds directly into your market-based Scope 2
            calculation — we integrate both.
files_2-irec.i_rec_retirement_data_is_integrated_into:
I-REC retirement data is integrated into your annual GHG inventory, CDP
                Climate response and BRSR electricity reporting — ensuring consistency across all disclosure channels.
files_2-irec.i_recs_are_the_global_standard_for_track:
I-RECs are the global standard for tracking and certifying renewable energy
            attributes. For organizations reporting Scope 2 emissions under the GHG Protocol, I-RECs are the recognized
            instrument for making market-based renewable energy claims.
files_2-irec.i_recs_satisfy_the_ghg_protocol_cdp_and_:
I-RECs satisfy the GHG Protocol, CDP and RE100 requirements for market-based Scope 2 — the
        globally recognised standard for renewable energy claims in India.
files_2-irec.if_you_own_or_operate_on_site_solar_gene:
If you own or operate on-site solar generation, you can use that electricity as market-based Scope 2
            without I-RECs — provided you retain the attribute (i.e., do not sell or transfer the certificate rights).
            If you sell the electricity or certificates, you must purchase I-RECs separately to make a market-based
            claim. For off-site renewable energy — which accounts for most corporate renewable electricity — I-RECs or
            PPAs are the required instrument. We help you design the most cost-effective combination of on-site
            generation and I-REC procurement for your Scope 2 target.
files_2-irec.integrate_retirement_data_into_ghg_inven:
Integrate retirement data into GHG inventory, CDP Scope 2 response, BRSR electricity
          section and investor communications.
files_2-irec.is_an_i_rec_the_same_as_a_carbon_credit: Is an I-REC the same as a carbon credit?
files_2-irec.location_based_scope_2_uses_the_average_:
Location-based Scope 2 uses the average emission factor of the electricity grid you are connected to — it
            reflects the carbon intensity of the physical grid. Market-based Scope 2 uses contractual instruments —
            PPAs, green tariffs, or energy attribute certificates like I-RECs — to reflect the specific electricity
            source your organisation has contracted. If you have retired I-RECs for renewable electricity you have
            purchased or generated, your market-based Scope 2 can be zero even if the grid is coal-heavy. CDP requires
            both to be reported; most investors focus on market-based.
files_2-irec.no_they_are_different_instruments_an_i_r:
No — they are different instruments. An I-REC (energy attribute certificate) documents the renewable
            origin of 1 MWh of electricity — it addresses Scope 2 electricity emissions by proving your electricity
            came from a clean source. A carbon credit documents the avoidance or removal of 1 tonne of CO2 equivalent
            — it is used to offset emissions that have already occurred. They have different registries, standards and
            accounting treatments. I-RECs reduce your market-based Scope 2 to zero for the electricity covered; carbon
            credits reduce your residual emissions after you have achieved deep reduction.
files_2-irec.pair_i_recs_for_scope_2_with_carbon_cred:
Pair I-RECs for Scope 2 with carbon credits for Scope 1 and 3 residual
            emissions for a complete net zero toolkit.
files_2-irec.public_i_rec_registry_records_providing_:
Public I-REC registry records providing permanent documentation of certificate
              issuance, transfer and retirement for investor and auditor verification.
files_2-irec.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_2-irec.source_i_recs_from_generators_or_registe:
Source I-RECs from generators or register your own facility for issuance — aligned to
          your CDP vintage, technology and geography requirements.
files_2-irec.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_2-irec.supplier_to_companies_requiring_renewabl: Supplier to companies requiring renewable electricity evidence
files_2-irec.the_ghg_protocol_scope_2_guidance_requir:
The GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance requires energy attribute certificates that meet
          specific quality criteria: issued for the same reporting period, retired exclusively on behalf of the
          company, and from a system that prevents double-counting. I-RECs satisfy all criteria.
files_2-irec.under_the_ghg_protocols_market_based_acc:
Under the GHG Protocol’s market-based accounting method, your Scope 2 renewable
      electricity claim is only valid if backed by a formally retired energy attribute certificate. An I-REC is that
      evidence.
files_2-irec.we_calculate_your_market_based_scope_2_e:
We calculate your market-based Scope 2 emissions using I-REC retirement data
                and residual emission factors — producing the CDP-compliant, GHG Protocol-aligned market-based Scope 2
                disclosure.
files_2-irec.we_execute_formal_i_rec_retirement_in_th:
We execute formal I-REC retirement in the I-REC registry, generating the
                retirement certificates that substantiate your renewable electricity claims in CDP, GHG Protocol and
                BRSR disclosures.
files_2-irec.we_manage_every_step_of_the_i_rec_proces:
We manage every step of the I-REC process for both energy generators (issuance) and energy
        buyers (procurement and retirement).
files_2-irec.what_is_the_difference_between_location_: What is the difference between location-based and market-based Scope 2?

files_2-pcf.a_five_phase_pcf_programme_from_system_b:
A five-phase PCF programme from system boundary definition through to critical review and
      commercial deployment.
files_2-pcf.a_life_cycle_assessment_lca_per_iso_1404:
A Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) per ISO 14040/44 assesses multiple environmental impact categories across a
            product’s lifecycle: global warming potential, ozone depletion, eutrophication, acidification,
            resource depletion and more. A Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) per ISO 14067 is specifically scoped to
            climate impact only — it is essentially an LCA restricted to the greenhouse gas impact category. An EPD
            uses an LCA as its technical foundation and reports all impact categories including the PCF. For most
            commercial purposes, customers want the PCF (carbon data); for LEED/BREEAM compliance, they need the full
            EPD.
files_2-pcf.a_pcf_is_the_product_level_input_that_fe:
A PCF is the product-level input that feeds CBAM, Scope 3, EPD, carbon labels and low-carbon
        procurement — one study, multiple applications.
files_2-pcf.a_product_carbon_footprint_quantifies_gh:
A Product Carbon Footprint quantifies GHG emissions across a product’s full
            lifecycle — from raw material extraction through use and end-of-life.
files_2-pcf.an_epd_uses_the_pcf_lca_as_its_technical:
An EPD uses the PCF LCA as its technical foundation — we often develop both
            in parallel for manufacturing clients.
files_2-pcf.business_seeking_carbon_label_for_produc: Business seeking carbon label for products
files_2-pcf.business_targeting_b2b_sustainability_pr: Business targeting B2B sustainability procurement requirements
files_2-pcf.calculate_all_ghg_emission_categories_us:
Calculate all GHG emission categories using ecoinvent/GaBi with primary data substitution
          and uncertainty analysis.
files_2-pcf.carbon_label_design_and_usage_guidelines:
Carbon label design and usage guidelines for product packaging, data sheets,
              marketing materials and B2B procurement responses.
files_2-pcf.cbam_requires_embedded_emission_intensit:
CBAM requires embedded emission intensity per tonne of exported product — which is
          essentially a PCF. An ISO 14067 PCF provides the methodological rigour that EU importers can use for CBAM
          compliance with greater credibility than CBAM default values.
files_2-pcf.collect_production_data_from_your_facili:
Collect production data from your facility — energy, materials, transport, waste — using
          structured templates.
files_2-pcf.company_developing_epd_for_construction_: Company developing EPD for construction products
files_2-pcf.company_exporting_to_eu_needing_cbam_dat: Company exporting to EU needing CBAM data
files_2-pcf.consumer_and_b2b_markets_are_moving_towa:
Consumer and B2B markets are moving toward carbon-labelled products in food,
          packaging, construction and consumer electronics. A credible PCF is the prerequisite for any carbon label or
          “low carbon” product claim.
files_2-pcf.coordinate_independent_lca_expert_review:
Coordinate independent LCA expert review, respond to queries and revise through to
          approved review statement.
files_2-pcf.define_functional_unit_system_boundary_c:
Define functional unit, system boundary, cut-off rules and allocation approach per ISO
          14067 requirements.
files_2-pcf.eu_customers_are_under_legal_obligation_:
EU customers are under legal obligation to account for Scope 3 emissions in their supply chain.
      Your product’s carbon footprint is their Category 1 data. Without a PCF, you make them estimate — and
      estimates are usually pessimistic.
files_2-pcf.from_lca_to_verified_carbon_label: from LCA to verified carbon label
files_2-pcf.full_product_carbon_footprint_report_doc:
Full Product Carbon Footprint report documenting system boundary, data sources,
              calculation methodology, uncertainty analysis and results per impact category.
files_2-pcf.how_accurate_does_a_pcf_need_to_be: How accurate does a PCF need to be?
files_2-pcf.how_many_pcfs_do_we_need_and_for_which_p: How many PCFs do we need and for which products?
files_2-pcf.iso_14067_requires_explicit_uncertainty_:
ISO 14067 requires explicit uncertainty analysis — quantifying how uncertain your result is. At first
            assessment using primary production data supplemented by secondary databases (ecoinvent, DEFRA), typical
            uncertainty is ±10-15%, which is sufficient for disclosure and benchmarking. Accuracy improves each cycle
            as primary supplier data replaces secondary database estimates. For CBAM purposes, your PCF’s
            embedded emission intensity needs to be defensible in an EU audit — we design data collection to meet this
            standard from the outset.
files_2-pcf.manufacturer_aiming_for_low_carbon_produ: Manufacturer aiming for low-carbon product differentiation
files_2-pcf.manufacturer_with_eu_customer_scope_3_re: Manufacturer with EU customer Scope 3 requirements
files_2-pcf.organisation_responding_to_supply_chain_: Organisation responding to supply chain PCF questionnaires
files_2-pcf.pcf_data_formatted_for_eu_customer_scope:
PCF data formatted for EU customer Scope 3 Category 1 reporting, CDP supply chain
              response and CBAM embedded emission reporting.
files_2-pcf.pcf_embedded_emissions_data_feeds_direct:
PCF embedded emissions data feeds directly into CBAM quarterly reporting —
            one methodology serves both.
files_2-pcf.pcfs_are_calculated_per_product_category:
PCFs are calculated per product category — each distinct product or product group with a materially
            different production process or raw material mix requires a separate PCF. For manufacturers with large
            product ranges, we recommend starting with your highest-volume EU-exported products (CBAM priority), your
            most carbon-intensive products (highest reduction opportunity) and your flagship products for marketing
            use. We typically develop a multi-product PCF programme in phases, starting with the highest-impact
            subset.
files_2-pcf.pcfs_are_increasingly_required_by_eu_cus:
PCFs are increasingly required
              by EU customers, enable CBAM compliance and underpin carbon labelling claims.
files_2-pcf.pcfs_require_critical_review_by_an_indep:
PCFs require critical review by an independent LCA expert. We coordinate the
                review, respond to reviewer queries and manage revision through to a signed critical review statement
                that validates your PCF’s credibility.
files_2-pcf.primary_data_is_supplemented_by_secondar:
Primary data is supplemented by secondary emission factors from
                peer-reviewed databases — ecoinvent, GaBi, DEFRA — with explicit documentation of each factor source,
                version and applicability.
files_2-pcf.prioritised_improvement_opportunities_ra:
Prioritised improvement opportunities — raw material substitution, energy
              efficiency, transport optimisation — ranked by carbon reduction potential per investment.
files_2-pcf.produce_iso_14067_pcf_report_carbon_labe:
Produce ISO 14067 PCF report, carbon label assets and customer data submission in
          required formats.
files_2-pcf.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_2-pcf.signed_critical_review_statement_from_in:
Signed critical review statement from independent LCA expert panel — required for
              third-party claim validation and investor/customer verification.
files_2-pcf.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_2-pcf.visual_lifecycle_hotspot_analysis_identi:
Visual lifecycle hotspot analysis identifying the life cycle stages and input
              categories with the highest carbon contribution — directing your reduction investment.
files_2-pcf.we_build_product_carbon_footprints_that_:
We build Product Carbon Footprints that satisfy ISO 14067, GHG Protocol Product Standard, EU
        customer requirements and CBAM methodology simultaneously.
files_2-pcf.we_collect_primary_production_data_from_:
We collect primary production data from your facility — raw material inputs,
                energy consumption, process emissions, transport, waste and water — through structured questionnaires
                and ERP data extraction.
files_2-pcf.we_define_the_functional_unit_system_bou:
We define the functional unit, system boundary (cradle-to-gate,
                cradle-to-grave or cradle-to-cradle) and cut-off rules per ISO 14067 and GHG Protocol Product Standard
                — aligned to your customer’s specific requirements.
files_2-pcf.we_deliver_iso_14067_aligned_pcfs_that_s:
We deliver ISO
            14067-aligned PCFs that stand up to third-party scrutiny.
files_2-pcf.we_identify_the_emission_hotspots_in_you:
We identify the emission hotspots in your product lifecycle — typically raw
                materials and energy for manufacturers — providing the intelligence needed to target reduction
                investments with the highest carbon impact per investment.
files_2-pcf.what_is_in_your_products_footprint: what is in your product’s footprint.
files_2-pcf.what_is_the_difference_between_a_pcf_and: What is the difference between a PCF and an LCA?
files_2-pcf.your_corporate_ghg_inventory_and_your_pc:
Your corporate GHG inventory and your PCF use the same emission factors — we
            integrate both to eliminate duplication.
files_2-pcf.your_eu_us_and_japanese_customers_need_p:
Your EU, US and Japanese customers need primary PCF data from suppliers to manage
          their Scope 3 Category 1 (purchased goods and services). A verified PCF replaces their rough estimates — and
          typically shows lower emissions, reducing their reported Scope 3 burden.

files_3-ecovadis.12_month_action_plan_targeting_the_next_:
12-month action plan targeting the next medal tier, with prioritised initiatives, owners
          and timelines aligned to your next annual re-assessment window.
files_3-ecovadis.45th54th_percentile_above_average_perfor:
45th–54th percentile. Above-average performance. Required by most tier-1 global
          buyers as minimum qualification.
files_3-ecovadis.4_core_policies_drafted_to_ecovadis_stan:
4 core policies drafted to EcoVadis standards: Environmental Policy, Human Rights
              & Labour Policy, Ethics & Anti-Corruption Policy, Sustainable Procurement Policy.
files_3-ecovadis.a_formal_materiality_process_strengthens:
A formal materiality process strengthens EcoVadis’s governance and
            management system criteria — improving scores in all four themes.
files_3-ecovadis.a_six_phase_programme_covering_pre_submi:
A six-phase programme covering pre-submission preparation, questionnaire management, and
      post-result improvement planning.
files_3-ecovadis.accelerate_your_journey_to_a_platinum_me:
Accelerate your journey to a Platinum
              medal.
files_3-ecovadis.an_epd_with_verified_lca_data_supports_e:
An EPD with verified LCA data supports EcoVadis Environment theme scoring and
            satisfies EU buyer product-level sustainability requirements.
files_3-ecovadis.attr_alt_ecovadis_documentation_audit_an: EcoVadis documentation audit and evidence review
files_3-ecovadis.attr_alt_ecovadis_strategy_and_sustainab: EcoVadis Strategy and sustainability rating assessment
files_3-ecovadis.below_45th_percentile_high_supply_chain_:
Below 45th percentile. High supply chain risk. Buyers actively seek alternatives.
          Urgent improvement required.
files_3-ecovadis.business_receiving_first_ecovadis_invita: Business receiving first EcoVadis invitation — no prior experience
files_3-ecovadis.collection_formatting_and_quality_review:
Collection, formatting and quality review of all supporting evidence documents aligned to
          EcoVadis reviewer expectations per theme.
files_3-ecovadis.company_seeking_esg_linked_supply_chain_: Company seeking ESG-linked supply chain financing
files_3-ecovadis.company_stuck_below_silver_losing_procur: Company stuck below Silver — losing procurement opportunities
files_3-ecovadis.complete_organised_evidence_file_for_all:
Complete, organised evidence file for all 21 criteria, formatted for EcoVadis
              reviewer requirements with a document index and cross-reference table.
files_3-ecovadis.detailed_scorecard_debrief_identifying_e:
Detailed scorecard debrief identifying exact scoring gaps per theme and criterion,
          benchmarked against sector peers and medal tier thresholds.
files_3-ecovadis.does_ecovadis_accept_iso_14001_and_sa800: Does EcoVadis accept ISO 14001 and SA8000 certifications as evidence?
files_3-ecovadis.drafting_or_strengthening_of_required_po:
Drafting or strengthening of required policies — environmental, labour, ethics,
          sustainable procurement — to EcoVadis scoring standards.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_is_the_worlds_most_trusted_prov:
EcoVadis is the world's most trusted provider of business sustainability
            ratings. Organizations use it to monitor the ESG performance of their global supply chain, and for
            suppliers,
            a high EcoVadis medal is a significant competitive advantage.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_medals_are_awarded_based_on_per:
EcoVadis medals are awarded based on percentile rank within your industry, size and geography category
            — not absolute score alone. In practice: Silver requires approximately the 45th–54th
            percentile (roughly a score of 45–54 out of 100), Gold requires the top 25% (score approximately
            55–74) and Platinum requires the top 1% (score approximately 75+). Exact thresholds vary slightly by
            category. What this means practically is that the same absolute score can achieve different medals
            depending on your sector’s average performance — which is why our pre-submission gap analysis
            benchmarks against your specific EcoVadis peer group, not a generic score target.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_scorecards_are_valid_for_12_mon:
EcoVadis scorecards are valid for 12 months from the date of issue. After 12 months, your scorecard
            expires and you must complete a new assessment to maintain your medal status and share it with buyers.
            Many buyers with EcoVadis procurement requirements will delist suppliers whose scorecard has lapsed. We
            track your renewal window and begin re-assessment preparation 3 months before expiry to ensure there is no
            gap in your medal status — particularly important for suppliers to buyers who conduct real-time
            scorecard validity checks.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_scores_across_environment_labou:
EcoVadis scores across Environment, Labour & Human Rights, Ethics and Sustainable
        Procurement — criteria that overlap significantly with GRI, ISO 14001, SA8000 and the UN Global Compact.
        Our preparation work builds documentation that serves EcoVadis and simultaneously strengthens your broader ESG
        programme.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_scores_range_from_0100_across_f:
EcoVadis scores range from 0–100 across four themes. Medals are awarded based on
      percentile rank within your industry and size category.
files_3-ecovadis.ecovadis_weights_the_four_themes_differe:
EcoVadis weights the four themes differently based on your industry’s risk profile. Environment
            typically carries 30–35% weight for manufacturing and industrial companies, Labour & Human
            Rights 25–30%, Ethics 15–20% and Sustainable Procurement 10–15%. However, for high-risk
            procurement categories — such as textiles, electronics and chemicals — Labour & Human
            Rights can be weighted as heavily as Environment. Our gap analysis identifies your specific weighting
            profile so we can prioritise effort where it produces the largest score improvement. Improving your
            weakest theme by 10 points typically yields far more total score improvement than marginal gains on your
            strongest theme.
files_3-ecovadis.eu_buyers_facing_csrd_scope_3_reporting_:
EU buyers facing CSRD Scope 3 reporting obligations are increasingly using EcoVadis
          Gold and Platinum status as a proxy for supplier emissions data quality. A high EcoVadis score directly
          reduces your EU customer’s ESG compliance burden.
files_3-ecovadis.expert_guided_questionnaire_completion_a:
Expert-guided questionnaire completion and final review before submission. Every answer
          is calibrated to maximise scoring under EcoVadis criteria.
files_3-ecovadis.expert_guided_questionnaire_completion_e:
Expert-guided questionnaire completion ensuring every question is answered
                at the highest defensible level, with supporting evidence cross-referenced correctly to prevent
                scoring penalties from submission errors.
files_3-ecovadis.exporter_facing_eu_csrd_scope_3_supply_c: Exporter facing EU CSRD Scope 3 supply chain disclosure pressure
files_3-ecovadis.full_21_criteria_gap_assessment_against_:
Full 21-criteria gap assessment against current documentation and practices. Score
          simulation to establish your improvement potential and target medal tier.
files_3-ecovadis.full_compilation_of_supporting_evidence_:
Full compilation of supporting evidence — certificates, KPI data,
                audit reports, training records, third-party verifications — organised in the exact format
                EcoVadis reviewers expect.
files_3-ecovadis.fully_completed_ecovadis_questionnaire_r:
Fully completed EcoVadis questionnaire, reviewed for scoring accuracy before
              submission, with every supporting document correctly attached per EcoVadis requirements.
files_3-ecovadis.global_procurement_teams_use_ecovadis_as:
Global procurement teams use EcoVadis as a mandatory supplier qualification gate. A score below
      Silver increasingly means exclusion from tender shortlists — regardless of price or quality.
files_3-ecovadis.how_long_does_the_ecovadis_assessment_ta: How long does the EcoVadis assessment take?
files_3-ecovadis.in_commodity_sectors_where_price_competi:
In commodity sectors where price competition is intense, an EcoVadis Gold or
          Platinum medal is a genuine differentiator that justifies price premiums and secures preferred supplier
          relationships with sustainability-committed buyers.
files_3-ecovadis.iso_14001_and_45001_certifications_are_a:
ISO 14001 and 45001 certifications are among the most efficient ways to
            improve your EcoVadis Environment and Labour theme scores.
files_3-ecovadis.organisation_preparing_for_annual_ecovad: Organisation preparing for annual EcoVadis re-assessment
files_3-ecovadis.organisation_targeting_gold_or_platinum_: Organisation targeting Gold or Platinum for commercial advantage
files_3-ecovadis.over_100000_buyer_companies_globally_req:
Over 100,000 buyer companies globally require EcoVadis as part of supplier
          qualification. A score below their threshold — typically Silver — results in disqualification
          from procurement processes regardless of pricing or quality.
files_3-ecovadis.post_result_analysis_of_your_scorecard_b:
Post-result analysis of your scorecard by theme and criterion, with peer
              benchmarking and a prioritised list of improvement actions for the next re-assessment cycle.
files_3-ecovadis.post_result_scorecard_analysis_identifyi:
Post-result scorecard analysis identifying the exact criteria preventing the
                next medal tier, with a 12-month improvement roadmap targeting your next annual re-assessment.
files_3-ecovadis.pre_submission_analysis_estimating_your_:
Pre-submission analysis estimating your likely score across all 4 themes,
              identifying the specific criteria creating the largest gaps to your target medal.
files_3-ecovadis.preparing_a_strong_first_ecovadis_submis:
Preparing a strong first EcoVadis submission typically takes 8–12 weeks from kickoff to submission,
            depending on how much documentation already exists in your organisation. EcoVadis then takes 4–6
            weeks after submission to issue your scorecard. Our preparation phase covers gap analysis (1–2
            weeks), policy development (3–4 weeks), evidence compilation (2–3 weeks) and questionnaire
            completion (1–2 weeks). For re-assessments where policies are already in place, preparation time
            typically reduces to 4–6 weeks focused on updating evidence and targeting scoring improvements from
            the previous cycle.
files_3-ecovadis.prioritised_action_plan_targeting_the_ne:
Prioritised action plan targeting the next medal tier: specific initiatives,
              document gaps, process changes and training requirements with timelines and ownership.
files_3-ecovadis.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_3-ecovadis.several_major_banks_and_financial_instit:
Several major banks and financial institutions in India and Europe now offer
          preferential rates on supply chain finance, working capital facilities and green bonds to suppliers with
          EcoVadis Silver or above. Gold and Platinum unlock premium tier pricing.
files_3-ecovadis.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_3-ecovadis.supplier_to_a_global_buyer_requiring_eco: Supplier to a global buyer requiring EcoVadis registration
files_3-ecovadis.top_1_globally_sustainability_leadership:
Top 1% globally. Sustainability leadership. Opens premium contract opportunities and
          significantly strengthens CBAM and CSRD positioning.
files_3-ecovadis.top_25_globally_strong_sustainability_ma:
Top 25% globally. Strong sustainability management. Preferred supplier status with
          leading multinationals and EU buyers.
files_3-ecovadis.we_conduct_a_full_pre_submission_gap_ana:
We conduct a full pre-submission gap analysis against all 21 EcoVadis
                criteria, simulate your likely score based on current documentation and identify the highest-impact
                improvement opportunities before submission.
files_3-ecovadis.we_draft_or_strengthen_the_policies_ecov:
We draft or strengthen the policies EcoVadis weighs most heavily —
                environmental policy, supplier code of conduct, human rights policy, anti-corruption measures —
                mapped precisely to EcoVadis scoring criteria.
files_3-ecovadis.we_manage_the_complete_ecovadis_cycle_in:
We manage the complete EcoVadis cycle: initial assessment, score improvement roadmap,
        document preparation, questionnaire submission and annual re-assessment — with a track record of moving
        clients up one or two medal tiers.
files_3-ecovadis.what_score_do_i_need_for_silver_gold_and: What score do I need for Silver, Gold and Platinum?
files_3-ecovadis.which_ecovadis_theme_has_the_highest_wei: Which EcoVadis theme has the highest weight?
files_3-ecovadis.yes_third_party_certifications_like_iso_:
Yes — third-party certifications like ISO 14001, ISO 50001, SA8000, ISO 45001 and OHSAS 18001 carry
            significant weight in EcoVadis scoring and are among the most efficient ways to improve specific theme
            scores. An ISO 14001 certificate can boost your Environment theme substantially; SA8000 does the same for
            Labour. However, certifications alone are not sufficient for Gold or Platinum — EcoVadis also
            evaluates your policies, KPI data, action plans and supplier management practices. We develop a
            certification–policy–data triangle for each theme to maximise your total score, not just rely
            on certificates.

files_3-epd.a_five_phase_process_from_pcr_identifica:
A five-phase process from PCR identification to published EPD, typically completed in
      10–16 weeks depending on product complexity and data availability.
files_3-epd.a_product_carbon_footprint_pcf_quantifie:
A Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) quantifies only the greenhouse gas emissions (GWP) associated with a
            product across its lifecycle — expressed in kg CO²e per functional unit. An EPD is broader: it
            reports 15+ environmental impact categories including GWP, acidification, eutrophication, ozone depletion,
            photochemical oxidant formation and resource depletion, all verified by a third party and published under
            an accredited Programme Operator. An EPD contains the PCF data as one of its impact categories. For CBAM
            purposes, the GWP data from an EPD can directly support embedded emissions declarations, but CBAM requires
            installation-level verified data rather than product-category averages.
files_3-epd.a_verified_epd_significantly_strengthens:
A verified EPD significantly strengthens EcoVadis Environment theme scoring
            — one of the most efficient paths to Gold or Platinum.
files_3-epd.an_environmental_product_declaration_epd:
An Environmental Product Declaration (EPD) provides verified, transparent
            information about the environmental impact of a product across its entire lifecycle. Based on ISO 14025,
            EPDs are the gold standard for green procurement.
files_3-epd.an_epd_is_a_third_party_verified_quantit:
An EPD is a third-party verified, quantitative sustainability claim that goes far
          beyond marketing language. It enables you to make specific, credible statements about your product’s
          environmental performance — and prevents greenwashing accusations.
files_3-epd.an_epd_quantifies_environmental_impacts_:
An EPD quantifies environmental impacts across the full product lifecycle in accordance with
      ISO 14044 LCA methodology and the applicable Product Category Rules (PCR).
files_3-epd.an_epd_with_a_verified_lca_produces_the_:
An EPD with a verified LCA produces the product-level embedded emissions data that
          CBAM-regulated exporters need. It also forms the foundation for a Product Carbon Footprint (PCF)
          certification and satisfies CSRD Scope 3 supplier data requests.
files_3-epd.attr_alt_environmental_product_declarati: Environmental Product Declaration and sustainable material certification
files_3-epd.attr_alt_lca_data_analysis_for_environme: LCA data analysis for environmental product declarations
files_3-epd.business_seeking_to_substantiate_green_p: Business seeking to substantiate green product claims under ISO 14021
files_3-epd.buyer_facing_epd_summary_sheet_key_impac:
Buyer-facing EPD summary sheet, key impact figures formatted for tenders and
              procurement responses, and guidance on using EPD claims in compliance with ISO 14021 green claims rules.
files_3-epd.can_one_epd_cover_multiple_product_varia: Can one EPD cover multiple product variants?
files_3-epd.company_competing_in_eu_public_procureme: Company competing in EU public procurement tenders requiring EPDs
files_3-epd.company_responding_to_csrd_scope_3_produ: Company responding to CSRD Scope 3 product emissions data requests
files_3-epd.coordination_of_the_mandatory_iso_14044_:
Coordination of the mandatory ISO 14044-required critical review by an
                independent, qualified LCA practitioner or review panel — a prerequisite for EPD registration
                and publication.
files_3-epd.deconstruction_transport_to_waste_facili:
Deconstruction, transport to waste facility, waste processing and disposal
          impacts.
files_3-epd.epd_document_preparation_to_programme_op:
EPD document preparation to Programme Operator template, independent critical review
          coordination and resolution of all review comments.
files_3-epd.epds_are_valid_for_5_years_from_the_date:
EPDs are valid for 5 years from the date of publication by the Programme Operator. After expiry, the EPD
            is withdrawn from the active registry. To maintain an active EPD, you must update the underlying LCA study
            and resubmit for a new 5-year registration. The update process is substantially faster than a first EPD
            — typically 4–8 weeks — because the modelling framework and data collection templates
            already exist. We track your EPD expiry dates and begin renewal preparation at least 3 months in advance
            to ensure continuous availability for procurement responses.
files_3-epd.epds_produced_under_iso_14025_and_en_158:
EPDs produced under ISO 14025 and EN 15804 are accepted by all major green building
        certification schemes, EU procurement frameworks and supply chain sustainability standards worldwide.
files_3-epd.exporter_to_the_eu_facing_product_level_:
Exporter to the EU facing product-level environmental documentation
            requirements
files_3-epd.full_iso_14044_compliant_lca_covering_al:
Full ISO 14044-compliant LCA covering all relevant lifecycle stages. We
                collect primary data from your production process and supplement with ecoinvent background data,
                quantifying 15+ environmental impact categories including GWP, AP, EP, ODP and POCP.
files_3-epd.full_iso_14044_compliant_lca_model_build:
Full ISO 14044-compliant LCA model build, impact assessment across 15+ categories and
          sensitivity/uncertainty analysis per PCR requirements.
files_3-epd.green_building_projects_eu_public_procur:
Green building projects, EU public procurement and global buyers increasingly require EPDs as
      mandatory documentation — and the EU Green Deal is accelerating this across manufacturing sectors.
files_3-epd.how_long_is_an_epd_valid_and_what_happen: How long is an EPD valid and what happens when it expires?
files_3-epd.identification_of_the_lifecycle_stages_a:
Identification of the lifecycle stages and material inputs contributing most to
              each impact category — the foundation for a product-level decarbonisation strategy.
files_3-epd.in_use_emissions_energy_and_water_use_ma:
In-use emissions, energy and water use, maintenance, repair and refurbishment over
          service life.
files_3-epd.independent_expert_critical_review_of_th:
Independent expert critical review of the LCA study as required by ISO 14044 and
              Programme Operator rules, confirming methodological conformance.
files_3-epd.iso_14025_compliant_epd_registered_with_:
ISO 14025-compliant EPD registered with an accredited Programme Operator,
              published on the global EPD registry with a unique registration number. Valid for 5 years.
files_3-epd.iso_14044_compliant_life_cycle_assessmen:
ISO 14044-compliant Life Cycle Assessment study covering all lifecycle stages,
              impact categories and data quality assessment — the technical foundation for the EPD.
files_3-epd.leed_v4_breeam_griha_and_green_star_all_:
LEED v4+, BREEAM, GRIHA and Green Star all award credits for products with verified
          EPDs. Developers specifying products for certified buildings actively require EPDs from manufacturers
          — and will substitute suppliers who cannot provide them.
files_3-epd.let_your_products_sustainability_be_veri:
Let your product's sustainability be verified
              by science.
files_3-epd.manufacturer_supplying_to_green_building: Manufacturer supplying to green building projects (LEED / BREEAM)
files_3-epd.net_benefits_from_reuse_recovery_and_rec:
Net benefits from reuse, recovery and recycling potential beyond the system
          boundary.
files_3-epd.organisation_targeting_ecovadis_gold_or_: Organisation targeting EcoVadis Gold or Platinum
files_3-epd.preparation_of_the_epd_document_in_progr:
Preparation of the EPD document in Programme Operator format, covering all
                mandatory content: product description, system boundary, data quality assessment, LCA results table,
                and all required EN 15804 + A2 impact category declarations.
files_3-epd.primary_production_data_collection_from_:
Primary production data collection from your facility (energy, materials, water, waste,
          transport) and background data integration from ecoinvent and regional databases.
files_3-epd.product_type_classification_pcr_identifi:
Product type classification, PCR identification, Programme Operator selection, system
          boundary definition and functional unit declaration.
files_3-epd.programme_operator_submission_registrati:
Programme Operator submission, registration fee management, final publication on the EPD
          registry and delivery of the verified, publication-ready EPD document.
files_3-epd.raw_material_extraction_transport_to_fac: Raw material extraction, transport to factory, and manufacturing process impacts.
files_3-epd.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_3-epd.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_3-epd.steel_cement_or_aluminium_producer_with_: Steel, cement or aluminium producer with CBAM-regulated exports
files_3-epd.submission_to_and_registration_with_your:
Submission to and registration with your chosen accredited Programme
                Operator, resulting in a published EPD with a unique registration number valid for five years and
                accessible to buyers globally via the EPD registry.
files_3-epd.the_choice_of_programme_operator_po_depe:
The choice of Programme Operator (PO) depends on your target markets, product type and applicable Product
            Category Rules. IBU (Institut Bauen und Umwelt) is strongest for the German and Central European
            construction market. EPD International (The International EPD System) has the broadest global recognition.
            GreenTag is widely recognised in Australia and the Asia-Pacific region. BRE is strong for BREEAM-focused
            UK and European projects. We recommend the PO based on where your primary buyers are located and which
            green building schemes they specify — and in many cases we recommend registering with two POs
            simultaneously to maximise market coverage.
files_3-epd.the_eus_green_public_procurement_gpp_cri:
The EU’s Green Public Procurement (GPP) criteria and Construction Products
          Regulation increasingly require EPDs for products supplied to EU public sector projects. Indian exporters of
          steel, cement, aluminium and building materials are directly affected.
files_3-epd.the_gwp_data_from_your_epd_directly_supp:
The GWP data from your EPD directly supports CBAM embedded emissions
            declarations for EU exports of steel, aluminium, cement and fertilisers.
files_3-epd.the_lca_hotspot_analysis_from_your_epd_i:
The LCA hotspot analysis from your EPD identifies the highest-impact
            reduction opportunities to inform your SBTi decarbonisation pathway.
files_3-epd.transport_to_site_and_installation_or_as: Transport to site and installation or assembly process environmental loads.
files_3-epd.verified_global_warming_potential_data_p:
Verified Global Warming Potential data per functional unit usable directly for
              CBAM embedded emissions declarations, CSRD Scope 3 supplier data requests and PCF certification.
files_3-epd.we_identify_the_applicable_pcr_for_your_:
We identify the applicable PCR for your product type, select the appropriate
                Programme Operator (IBU, EPD International, GreenTag, BRE) and define the system boundary, functional
                unit and scope of the LCA study.
files_3-epd.we_manage_the_complete_epd_development_p:
We manage the complete EPD development process: LCA study, EPD drafting, third-party critical
        review, Programme Operator registration and publication on the global EPD registry — delivering a
        verified, internationally recognised EPD for your product.
files_3-epd.what_is_the_difference_between_an_epd_an: What is the difference between an EPD and a product carbon footprint?
files_3-epd.which_programme_operator_should_we_regis: Which Programme Operator should we register with?
files_3-epd.yes_under_most_pcrs_a_single_epd_can_dec:
Yes — under most PCRs, a single EPD can declare results for multiple product variants (different
            grades, thicknesses, compositions) within the same product family, provided the variants share the same
            functional unit and system boundary. The EPD then declares separate LCA results for each variant. This is
            significantly more cost-efficient than producing individual EPDs per variant and is the approach we
            recommend for manufacturers with product ranges. Some PCRs do restrict this — particularly EN 15804
            for construction products — and we check applicability during the scoping phase before committing to
            a product range approach.

For CSRD-applicable organisations, we conduct the full impact materiality
                assessment — evaluating the severity, scope and likelihood of your operations’ actual and
                potential impacts on people and environment.
files_3-materiality.governance_protocol_for_monitoring_when_:
Governance protocol for monitoring when material topics require re-assessment,
              with trigger criteria for interim updates ahead of the full 3-year review cycle.
files_3-materiality.gri_2021_does_not_prescribe_a_fixed_refr:
GRI 2021 does not prescribe a fixed refresh cycle, but the standard expectation — reinforced by
            ESRS — is a full reassessment every 2–3 years, with annual monitoring for significant changes.
            Triggers requiring an interim update include major M&A activity, entry into new geographies or
            business lines, significant regulatory changes, or material stakeholder incidents. We provide a monitoring
            protocol as part of every engagement so you always know when a refresh is due and what to watch for in the
            interim.

files_3-materiality.management_scoring_workshops_evaluating_:
Management scoring workshops evaluating each topic’s financial significance,

files_3-materiality.materiality_assessment_is_the_strategic_:
Materiality assessment is the strategic foundation of every credible ESG programme.
            It determines which sustainability topics are financially significant to your business and most important to
            your stakeholders — and
files_3-materiality.one_page_profile_per_material_topic_cove:
One-page profile per material topic covering definition, business rationale,

files_3-materiality.organisation_setting_up_sbti_or_net_zero: Organisation setting up SBTi or net-zero targets
files_3-materiality.our_materiality_methodology_is_designed_:
Our materiality methodology is designed from the ground up to simultaneously satisfy GRI
        2021, ESRS/CSRD, BRSR, ISSB and EcoVadis requirements — so one engagement drives compliance across your
        entire ESG disclosure programme.
files_3-materiality.our_standard_engagement_covers_four_stak:
Our standard engagement covers four stakeholder groups: internal (employees and senior management),
            commercial (customers and suppliers), financial (investors, lenders and analysts) and societal (local
            community representatives, NGOs and regulators where accessible). Within each group we target a
            statistically sufficient sample using structured digital surveys, supplemented by targeted interviews for
            key informants. For most engagements this means 50–200 survey respondents and 8–15 in-depth
            interviews. All responses are anonymised, weighted by group and documented for assurance purposes.
files_3-materiality.preparing_a_brsr_gri_or_csrd_report_for_: Preparing a BRSR, GRI or CSRD report for the first time
files_3-materiality.publication_ready_visual_matrix_plotting:
Publication-ready visual matrix plotting all assessed topics by stakeholder
              importance vs. business significance, with threshold lines and material topic callouts.
files_3-materiality.regulators_investors_and_frameworks_all_:
Regulators, investors and frameworks all require it. Without a rigorous, defensible materiality
      process, your sustainability disclosures lack credibility — and your strategy lacks direction.
files_3-materiality.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_3-materiality.science_based_targets_require_a_clear_pi:
Science-based targets require a clear picture of your material climate
            impacts — the output of a good materiality process.
files_3-materiality.sebis_brsr_framework_and_gri_universal_s:
SEBI’s BRSR framework and GRI Universal Standards 2021 both require a
          documented materiality process. Without it, your disclosure is structurally incomplete and vulnerable to
          regulatory and investor challenge.
files_3-materiality.sebis_brsr_framework_does_not_use_the_te:
SEBI’s BRSR framework does not use the term “materiality assessment” explicitly, but it
            requires disclosure on material risks, stakeholder engagement and the principle-wise assessment of the
            NGRBC 9 principles — all of which are substantively informed by a formal materiality process.
            Moreover, BRSR Core requires assured disclosures on KPIs that should be grounded in material topic
            identification. Investors reviewing BRSR quality increasingly expect to see a documented materiality
            process as evidence of genuine ESG governance rather than box-ticking. A GRI-aligned materiality
            assessment fully satisfies this expectation and strengthens your BRSR considerably.
files_3-materiality.single_materiality_used_by_brsr_gri_and_:
Single materiality (used by BRSR, GRI and ISSB) focuses on topics that are material to the financial
            performance and strategy of the company itself — essentially, what ESG factors affect your business.
            Double materiality (required by the EU’s CSRD and ESRS) adds a second dimension: the company’s
            own impacts on people and the environment, regardless of whether those impacts affect financial
            performance. CSRD-applicable companies must assess and disclose on both axes. We conduct both types and,
            where applicable, integrate them into a single assessment process to avoid duplication.
files_3-materiality.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_3-materiality.structured_online_surveys_and_targeted_i:
Structured online surveys and targeted interviews with internal and external stakeholder
          groups. Priority scoring with statistically valid weighting methodology.
files_3-materiality.structured_surveys_and_interviews_with_f:
Structured surveys and interviews with four key groups — employees,
                customers, investors and community representatives — to capture stakeholder priority scores with
                documented methodology.
files_3-materiality.the_eu_corporate_sustainability_reportin:
The EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive requires double materiality
          — assessing both financial materiality (impact on the company) and impact materiality (company’s
          impact on people and planet). Indian subsidiaries of EU companies face this obligation now.
files_3-materiality.we_build_a_comprehensive_universe_of_254:
We build a comprehensive universe of 25–40 candidate ESG topics drawn
                from GRI, ESRS, SASB and sector-specific risk libraries — tailored to your industry, geography
                and value chain.
files_3-materiality.we_conduct_end_to_end_materiality_assess:
We conduct end-to-end materiality assessments that meet the methodological requirements of
        GRI 2021, ESRS, BRSR and ISSB — producing a board-approved matrix and narrative your disclosures can
        reference for 2–3 years.
files_3-materiality.we_deliver_structured_board_approved_mat:
We
            deliver structured, board-approved materiality processes aligned to the latest GRI 2021, ESRS and ISSB
            standards.
files_3-materiality.we_produce_the_visual_materiality_matrix:
We produce the visual materiality matrix, a ranked list of material topics
                with methodology documentation, and a board-ready presentation that establishes governance sign-off
                for your ESG disclosures.
files_3-materiality.what_is_the_difference_between_single_an: What is the difference between single and double materiality?
files_3-materiality.yes_and_this_is_exactly_how_we_structure:
Yes — and this is exactly how we structure our engagements. A single well-designed materiality
            process, conducted to GRI 2021 standards with ESRS overlay, simultaneously satisfies the requirements of
            BRSR, GRI, CSRD, ISSB and EcoVadis. The key is building the topic universe to span all four
            frameworks’ topic lists from the start, and documenting methodology choices that meet each
            framework’s specific requirements. We deliver a cross-reference map showing how each material topic
            satisfies each applicable framework’s materiality disclosure requirement.
files_3-materiality.your_materiality_assessment_directly_dri:
Your materiality assessment directly drives BRSR principle-wise disclosures
            and risk reporting. We integrate both into a single programme.

BRSR Principle 6 on environmental responsibility and climate risk disclosure is

Full GHG Protocol-compliant inventory covering all material Scope 1, 2 and 3

Future-proof

Global buyers with their own SBTi commitments are setting Scope 3 supplier

Indirect emissions from the generation of purchased electricity, steam, heat or
          cooling consumed by the company.

Initiative-level action plan with emission reduction quantities per initiative,
            capital expenditure estimates, implementation timelines and annual KPIs for progress tracking.
files_3-sbti.initiative_level_action_plan_with_reduct:
Initiative-level action plan with reduction quantities, investment requirements,
            timelines and annual progress KPIs to track delivery against validated targets.
files_3-sbti.listed_company_building_credible_brsr_cl: Listed company building credible BRSR climate risk disclosures
files_3-sbti.materiality_screening_of_all_15_scope_3_:
Materiality screening of all 15 Scope 3 categories. Identification of material
            categories requiring full quantification vs. screening-level estimates.
files_3-sbti.modelling_of_near_term_and_long_term_tar:
Modelling of near-term and long-term target options using SBTi-approved
              methods (Absolute Contraction Approach, Sectoral Decarbonisation Approach, Economic Intensity
              Contraction). We identify the most achievable targets that still meet 1.5°C validation criteria.
files_3-sbti.near_term_and_long_term_targets_validate:
Near-term and long-term targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative
            and published on the SBTi website — the global credibility standard for 1.5°C alignment.
files_3-sbti.near_term_targets_cover_the_next_510_yea:
Near-term targets cover the next 5–10 years (typically to 2030) and require Scope 1+2 absolute
              reductions of at least 42% and Scope 3 reductions of at least 25% from a base year, consistent with a
              1.5°C pathway. Net-zero targets — validated under the SBTi Corporate Net-Zero Standard —
              require 90% absolute reduction in Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions by 2050 at the latest, with residual
              emissions addressed through permanent carbon removal (not offset credits). Near-term targets are
              mandatory; net-zero targets are best practice. We recommend setting both simultaneously, as the
              near-term target modelling is a direct input to the net-zero pathway design.
files_3-sbti.net_zero_standard_race_to_zero: Net Zero Standard — Race to Zero
files_3-sbti.organisation_seeking_cdp_a_list_or_msci_: Organisation seeking CDP A-List or MSCI AAA rating
files_3-sbti.post_validation_roadmap_identifying_spec:
Post-validation roadmap identifying specific emission reduction initiatives,
              capital investment requirements, timelines and KPIs to track annual progress against your validated SBTi
              targets — turning a commitment into an actionable internal programme.
files_3-sbti.preparation_of_the_sbti_commitment_lette:
Preparation of the SBTi commitment letter, target submission forms and all
              supporting documentation. We manage the SBTi online portal submission and respond to SBTi reviewer
              queries during the validation process.
files_3-sbti.right_for_your_organisation_if_you_are: Right for your organisation if you are…
files_3-sbti.sbti_requires_annual_public_progress_rep:
SBTi requires annual public progress reporting against your validated targets. If a company fails to
              make sufficient progress without a legitimate documented reason (such as major M&A restructuring),
              SBTi can revoke your validated target status and remove you from the SBTi website — a significant
              reputational consequence. This is why we build the decarbonisation roadmap as part of every engagement:
              targets that are validated but not backed by concrete, funded reduction initiatives create substantial
              delivery risk. Our roadmap identifies initiatives with sufficient combined reduction potential to hit
              near-term targets, with contingency initiatives built in for years where primary initiatives
              underperform.
files_3-sbti.sbti_requires_near_term_targets_510_year:
SBTi requires near-term targets (5–10 year horizon) and encourages long-term and
        net-zero targets for full science-alignment. We set all three in a single integrated framework.
files_3-sbti.sbti_requires_setting_targets_across_all:
SBTi requires setting targets across all three emission scopes. We build your GHG inventory and
      target framework covering every source material to your footprint.
files_3-sbti.sbti_sector_guidance_application_decarbo:
SBTi sector guidance application, decarbonisation pathway selection (ACA, SDA, EICA)
            and target ambition modelling against 1.5°C validation criteria.
files_3-sbti.sbti_sits_at_the_centre_of: SBTi sits at the centre of
files_3-sbti.sbti_sme_target_setting_tool_allows_smal:
SBTi SME target-setting tool allows smaller companies to commit to a standardised
            1.5°C target without full sector-specific pathway modelling — significantly reducing time and
            cost to commitment and validation.
files_3-sbti.sbti_validated_targets_directly_satisfy_:
SBTi-validated targets directly satisfy the most demanding requirements of CDP, TCFD, BRSR, GRI
      and ISSB S2 — anchoring your entire climate disclosure programme to a single, independently verified
      foundation.
files_3-sbti.sbti_validated_targets_significantly_str:
SBTi-validated targets significantly strengthen BRSR Principle 6 disclosures
          and provide the quantified climate commitments investors expect to see.
files_3-sbti.sbti_validation_confirmation_targets_pub:
SBTi validation confirmation, targets published on the SBTi website. Commitment and
            validation communications for investors, customers and ESG disclosures.
files_3-sbti.sbti_validation_independently_verifies_t:
SBTi validation independently verifies that your climate targets are genuinely
          aligned with limiting warming to 1.5°C — protecting you from the growing regulatory and
          reputational risk of greenwashing allegations against unverified net-zero claims.
files_3-sbti.scope_12_90_absolute_reduction_by_2050_s:
Scope 1+2: 90% absolute reduction by 2050. Scope 3: 90% absolute reduction.
            Residual emissions (max 10%) addressed through permanent carbon removal — not offset credits.
            Validated under SBTi Corporate Net-Zero Standard.
files_3-sbti.scope_12_minimum_42_absolute_reduction_b:
Scope 1+2: minimum 42% absolute reduction by 2030 from base year (1.5°C
            pathway). Scope 3: 25% absolute or 42% intensity reduction if Scope 3 >40% of total footprint. Must be
            submitted within 24 months of commitment.
files_3-sbti.scope_3_targets_are_required_only_if_sco:
Scope 3 targets are required only if Scope 3 emissions represent 40% or more of your total Scope 1+2+3
              footprint. For many manufacturing companies with significant direct energy use, Scope 1+2 dominate and
              Scope 3 targets are not required — though SBTi strongly encourages them. You can commit to SBTi
              before completing your full Scope 3 inventory, as you have 24 months after commitment to submit
              validated targets. However, we recommend completing the Scope 3 screening before committing, so you are
              not surprised by a mandatory Scope 3 target requirement. We conduct the full Scope 3 materiality
              screening as part of our kickoff phase.
files_3-sbti.screening_of_all_15_scope_3_categories_f:
Screening of all 15 Scope 3 categories for materiality, identification of your
              applicable SBTi sector guidance (steel, cement, apparel, ICT, buildings, transport etc.) and selection
              of the appropriate sector-specific decarbonisation pathway.
files_3-sbti.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_3-sbti.supplier_to_a_company_with_sbti_scope_3_: Supplier to a company with SBTi Scope 3 engagement requirement
files_3-sbti.the_ghg_inventory_built_for_sbti_provide:
The GHG inventory built for SBTi provides the verified embedded emissions data
          needed for CBAM compliance — one data collection serves both programmes.
files_3-sbti.the_science_based_targets_initiative_sbt:
The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) drives ambitious climate action
            by enabling organizations to set emissions reduction targets in line with a 1.5°C future.
files_3-sbti.this_is_one_of_the_most_important_distin:
This is one of the most important distinctions in corporate sustainability. “Carbon
              neutral” claims typically offset Scope 1 and 2 emissions using purchased carbon credits —
              without requiring any actual emission reductions. “Net-zero” without SBTi validation is an
              unverified claim with no standardised methodology. SBTi specifically requires actual emission reductions
              — not offset credits — to meet 42% Scope 1+2 reduction targets by 2030 and 90% by 2050.
              Carbon removal can address only residual emissions (maximum 10%) at net-zero, not replace the reduction
              requirement. This makes SBTi the only globally recognised standard that prevents “net-zero”
              from being used as a greenwashing shield without genuine decarbonisation.
files_3-sbti.we_manage_the_complete_sbti_journey_ghg_:
We manage the complete SBTi journey — GHG inventory, target modelling, commitment letter,
      target submission and post-validation progress reporting — so you reach SBTi validation with minimal
      internal burden.
files_3-sbti.what_happens_if_we_miss_our_sbti_targets: What happens if we miss our SBTi targets?
files_3-sbti.what_is_the_difference_between_sbti_near: What is the difference between SBTi near-term and net-zero targets?
files_3-sbti.without_sbti_validation_your_net_zero_or:
Without SBTi validation, your net-zero or carbon neutral claims are vulnerable to greenwashing
      accusations. With it, you have the world’s most credible independent climate commitment — recognised
      by investors, buyers and regulators.
files_3-sbti.yes_sbti_has_a_dedicated_sme_small_and_m:
Yes — SBTi has a dedicated SME (Small and Medium Enterprise) pathway that significantly
              simplifies the process for companies with revenues below €500 million. The SME fast-track uses a
              standardised commitment rather than full sector-specific target modelling, allowing SMEs to commit and
              have their targets validated much faster and at lower cost. The SME commitment requires agreeing to
              reduce Scope 1 and 2 emissions in half before 2030 and achieving net-zero before 2050 — a fixed
              commitment rather than a customised pathway. We guide Indian SMEs through the SME eligibility assessment
              and submission process, which is substantially faster than the full corporate pathway.

files_4-annual-env-statement.comparison_of_current_year_environmental:
Comparison of current year environmental performance against prior years —
            identifying trends that may attract GPCB attention or indicate deteriorating compliance.
files_4-annual-env-statement.comprehensive_gpcb_consent_management_ct:
Comprehensive GPCB consent management — CTE, CTO renewal, amendments and all
            statutory filings for industrial units in Gujarat.
files_4-annual-env-statement.customized_compliance_calendar_with_data:
Customized compliance calendar with data collection milestones, internal review
            dates and GPCB filing deadline to ensure no year is missed.
files_4-annual-env-statement.does_form_v_need_to_be_signed_by_a_direc: Does Form V need to be signed by a Director?
files_4-annual-env-statement.every_occupier_of_an_industry_or_operati:
Every occupier of an industry or operation that is covered under Schedule I of the Environment Protection
            Rules, 1986 and holds a GPCB consent is required to file Form V. This covers virtually all Red, Orange and
            Green category industries in Gujarat. GPCB's e-Gujcop portal now generates an automatic filing obligation
            for every CTO holder — making the filing visible to GPCB irrespective of whether you receive a reminder.
            Our standard service covers all GPCB-consented facilities of our clients.
files_4-annual-env-statement.expert_response_drafting_for_gpcb_show_c:
Expert response drafting for GPCB show-cause notices related to AES
            non-filing, environmental parameter exceedances and consent violations.
files_4-annual-env-statement.for_clients_with_prior_year_aes_gaps_we_:
For clients with prior-year AES gaps, we prepare and file all outstanding returns
            with a covering note to GPCB to regularize the compliance record before CTO renewal.
files_4-annual-env-statement.form_v_requires_data_across_six_key_area:
Form V requires data across six key areas: (1) water consumption by source and use — ground, surface,
            municipal; (2) wastewater generated, treated and discharged; (3) air emissions from stacks with pollutant
            loads; (4) solid waste generated, reused, recycled and disposed; (5) hazardous waste generated and
            disposed of with authorization details; (6) environmental capital expenditure and revenue expenditure for
            the year. We provide a structured data collection template that your environment officer or plant manager
            fills in — we then verify, calculate and populate Form V from that input.
files_4-annual-env-statement.gpcb_authorization_for_used_oil_generati:
GPCB authorization for used oil generation and disposal — a prerequisite for
            accurate hazardous waste reporting in your Annual Environmental Statement.
files_4-annual-env-statement.gpcb_verifies_aes_filing_history_as_part:
GPCB verifies AES filing history as part of CTO renewal scrutiny. Missing or late
          AES returns — even for years prior to the renewal period — create queries, delays and potentially adverse
          renewal conditions. Many companies discover historical AES gaps only when their renewal is held up.
files_4-annual-env-statement.gpcbs_inspection_scheduling_system_flags:
GPCB's inspection scheduling system flags units with missing or consistently late
          AES filings for priority site visits. An AES filing gap is effectively a signal to GPCB that a unit's
          environmental monitoring and recordkeeping practices need verification.
files_4-annual-env-statement.has_multiple_units_requiring_coordinated: Has multiple units requiring coordinated AES filing
files_4-annual-env-statement.has_never_filed_form_v_or_has_gaps_in_pr: Has never filed Form V or has gaps in prior-year filings
files_4-annual-env-statement.has_received_a_gpcb_query_or_notice_on_a: Has received a GPCB query or notice on AES non-filing
files_4-annual-env-statement.has_recently_started_operations_and_is_f: Has recently started operations and is filing Form V for the first time
files_4-annual-env-statement.holds_a_valid_gpcb_consent_to_operate_ct: Holds a valid GPCB Consent to Operate (CTO)
files_4-annual-env-statement.is_a_red_or_orange_category_industry_wit: Is a Red or Orange category industry with complex data requirements
files_4-annual-env-statement.is_approaching_cto_renewal_and_needs_a_c: Is approaching CTO renewal and needs a clean compliance record
files_4-annual-env-statement.lacks_internal_capacity_to_compile_envir: Lacks internal capacity to compile environmental monitoring data
files_4-annual-env-statement.non_filing_is_a_direct_consent_violation: Non-filing is a direct consent violation and frequently cited during CTO renewal scrutiny.
files_4-annual-env-statement.organized_archive_of_all_supporting_docu:
Organized archive of all supporting documents — monitoring reports, lab
            certificates, waste disposal manifests, energy bills — referenced to each AES parameter.
files_4-annual-env-statement.rule_14_of_the_environment_protection_ru:
Rule 14 of the Environment Protection Rules, 1986 imposes a direct legal obligation
          to submit Form V. Non-filing is an independent compliance violation — separate from consent conditions — and
          may attract prosecution under Section 15 of the EPA regardless of whether your consent is otherwise current.
files_4-annual-env-statement.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_4-annual-env-statement.structured_data_report_with_source_refer:
Structured data report with source references for each AES parameter — water,
            wastewater, air emissions, solid waste, hazardous waste and environmental expenditure.
files_4-annual-env-statement.submitted_annual_environmental_statement:
Submitted Annual Environmental Statement (Form V) with GPCB portal acknowledgement
            receipt for the reporting year.
files_4-annual-env-statement.the_aes_must_be_signed_by_an_authorized_:
The AES must be signed by an authorized signatory of the occupier — typically a
            Director or equivalent. We prepare a concise briefing document summarizing the key data points for the
            signatory review, ensuring informed sign-off well before the September 30 deadline.
files_4-annual-env-statement.the_aes_requires_specific_data_on_water_:
The AES requires specific data on water consumption, wastewater generation, air
          emission loads, solid and hazardous waste quantities and environmental expenditure. Inaccurate data —
          understating generation or overstating treatment — is treated as misrepresentation during audits and consent
          renewals.
files_4-annual-env-statement.the_annual_environmental_statement_aes_f:
The Annual Environmental Statement (AES) — Form V under the Environment Protection Rules, 1986 — is a
            mandatory statutory return that every GPCB-consented unit must file with GPCB by September 30 each year.
files_4-annual-env-statement.the_annual_environmental_statement_form_:
The Annual Environmental Statement (Form V) must be filed with GPCB by September 30 each year, covering
            environmental performance data for the preceding financial year (April 1 to March 31). GPCB does not
            typically grant extensions — late filing is treated as non-compliance. We initiate data collection in June
            to ensure adequate time for compilation, review and filing well before the deadline.
files_4-annual-env-statement.the_annual_environmental_statement_is_gp:
The Annual Environmental Statement is GPCB's primary tool for tracking a unit's environmental
      performance over time. Missing filings are among the most common deficiencies flagged during CTO renewal
      scrutiny.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_collect_and_verify_all_data_required_:
We collect and verify all data required for Form V — water drawn and consumed,
            wastewater generated and discharged, boiler and DG stack emissions data, solid waste quantities,
            hazardous waste generation and disposal, energy consumption, and capital/revenue expenditure on
            environmental compliance for the reporting year.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_handle_the_complete_aes_preparation_a:
We handle the complete AES preparation and filing process — from raw environmental data to
        submitted Form V with an acknowledgement.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_have_not_filed_form_v_for_the_past_3_: We have not filed Form V for the past 3 years — can we regularize?
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_prepare_accurate_audit_ready_aes_retu:
We prepare accurate, audit-ready AES returns from your environmental monitoring data — ensuring timely
            filing every year.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_prepare_the_complete_form_v_with_all_:
We prepare the complete Form V with all sections accurately populated —
            cross-referencing your monitoring records, laboratory analysis reports, purchase invoices and
            operational logs. We flag data gaps before submission rather than discovering them during GPCB queries.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_submit_the_form_v_on_the_gpcb_e_gujco:
We submit the Form V on the GPCB e-Gujcop portal, retain the submission
            acknowledgement, and maintain the complete supporting data archive — monitoring reports, laboratory
            certificates, waste disposal receipts and energy bills.
files_4-annual-env-statement.we_validate_your_aes_data_against_statut:
We validate your AES data against statutory records — including monitoring reports,
            laboratory analyses, manifests, and utility data — to ensure consistency across all submissions. We prepare
            your complete audit file and support you in responding to any GPCB queries, inspections.
files_4-annual-env-statement.what_data_do_i_need_to_provide_for_form_: What data do I need to provide for Form V preparation?
files_4-annual-env-statement.what_is_the_deadline_for_filing_the_annu: What is the deadline for filing the Annual Environmental Statement?
files_4-annual-env-statement.which_units_are_required_to_file_the_ann: Which units are required to file the Annual Environmental Statement?
files_4-annual-env-statement.yes_and_we_strongly_recommend_doing_so_b:
Yes — and we strongly recommend doing so before your next CTO renewal or any GPCB inspection. GPCB's
            e-Gujcop portal allows submission of prior-year returns, and filing them proactively — even if late —
            demonstrates good faith compliance intent. We prepare all outstanding Form V returns simultaneously, file
            them with a covering note to GPCB explaining the historical gap, and establish a forward-looking filing
            system to prevent future gaps.
files_4-annual-env-statement.yes_rule_14_of_the_environment_protectio:
Yes. Rule 14 of the Environment Protection Rules requires the Annual Environmental Statement to be signed
            by a responsible officer of the occupier — typically a Director, Managing Partner or Plant Head with
            authority to make statutory declarations on behalf of the organization. This is a legal declaration: the
            signatory is personally accountable for the accuracy of the information filed. We prepare a concise
            one-page summary of the key data for the signatory to review before signing, ensuring an informed and
            confident sign-off.
files_4-annual-env-statement.you_need_aes_preparation_if_your_facilit: You need AES preparation if your facility —

files_4-battery-waste.annual_compliance_calendar_with_deadline:
Annual compliance calendar with deadlines for target achievement, return filing and
            recycler certificate submission.
files_4-battery-waste.are_lead_acid_batteries_covered_under_th: Are lead-acid batteries covered under the Battery Waste Management Rules, 2022?
files_4-battery-waste.attr_alt_battery_waste_management_and_li: Battery waste management and lithium-ion recycling
files_4-battery-waste.authenticated_registration_certificate_f:
Authenticated registration certificate from the GPCB EPR portal covering all
            applicable battery categories.
files_4-battery-waste.automotive_oem_sourcing_lead_acid_or_lit: Automotive OEM sourcing lead-acid or lithium batteries
files_4-battery-waste.company_that_has_received_GPCB_notice_fo: Company that has received GPCB notice for EPR non-compliance
files_4-battery-waste.complete_documentary_evidence_package_in:
Complete documentary evidence package including weight slips, recycler audit reports
            and EPR certificate records for regulatory review.
files_4-battery-waste.comprehensive_gpcb_consent_management_ct:
Comprehensive GPCB consent management — CTE, CTO, renewals, amendments and
            hazardous waste authorizations for all industry categories in Gujarat.
files_4-battery-waste.do_i_need_epr_registration_if_i_sell_pro:
Do I need EPR registration if I sell products that contain batteries but am not a
          battery manufacturer?
files_4-battery-waste.epr_registration_and_annual_compliance_u:
EPR registration and annual compliance under the Plastic Waste Management
            Rules for producers, importers and brand owners of plastic packaging.
files_4-battery-waste.epr_targets_are_computed_as_a_percentage:
EPR targets are computed as a percentage of batteries placed in the market in a base year. The percentage
            escalates annually — starting at 30% in FY 2023-24 and rising to 70% by FY 2027-28 for most battery
            categories. Targets are separately computed for each of the four categories: portable, automotive,
            industrial and EV batteries. We compute your exact target based on your sales/import declarations and
            maintain the evidence chain to demonstrate target achievement.
files_4-battery-waste.ev_manufacturer_or_ev_battery_assembler: EV manufacturer or EV battery assembler
files_4-battery-waste.every_engagement_produces_a_complete_com: Every engagement produces a complete compliance dossier — not just a registration certificate.
files_4-battery-waste.every_producer_importer_and_refurbisher_:
