files_8-ambientair.24_hour_hi_vol_sampling_for_pm_pm_so_no_: 24-hour Hi-Vol sampling for PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, SO₂, NO₂, CO and additional parameters (Pb, HF, HCl, NH₃, VOCs as applicable) at all designated locations. Minimum 8 consecutive 24-hour sampling days per season per CPCB protocol for EIA baseline studies.
files_8-ambientair.24_hour_sampling_cycles_for_minimum_8_da: 24-hour sampling cycles for minimum 8 days per location per season. Daily field inspection, filter and impinger changes, field data recording and chain-of-custody documentation.
files_8-ambientair.a_five_stage_process_delivering_a_comple: A five-stage process delivering a complete, submission-ready ambient air quality monitoring report within 15–20 working days of sampling completion.
files_8-ambientair.accredited_accepted_by_all_regulators_co: Accredited — accepted by all regulators & courts
files_8-ambientair.air_dispersion_modelling_required_in_eia: Air dispersion modelling — required in EIA studies to predict ground-level concentrations from stack emissions — must be validated against measured ambient air quality data. Without measured baseline and downwind data, the modelling is unvalidated and vulnerable to challenge during public consultation and EAC review hearings.
files_8-ambientair.all_collected_filter_samples_analysed_at: All collected filter samples analysed at our NABL-accredited laboratory using gravimetric and wet chemical methods prescribed in IS 5182 and CPCB technical guidelines. Full QA/QC including field blanks, duplicates and spike recovery documentation.
files_8-ambientair.ambient_air_quality_baseline_data_is_man: Ambient air quality baseline data is mandatory for every EC application and half-yearly compliance report. We provide both the monitoring and the EC advisory in one integrated programme.
files_8-ambientair.ambient_air_quality_monitoring_measures_: Ambient air quality monitoring measures pollutant concentrations in the outdoor air around your facility boundary and in surrounding communities — comparing them against India’s National Ambient Air Quality (NAAQ) Standards.
files_8-ambientair.ambient_air_quality_monitoring_sustainco: Ambient Air Quality Monitoring — SustainCore Environmental Solutions
files_8-ambientair.ambient_and_stack_monitoring_together_pr: Ambient and stack monitoring together provide the complete regulatory air quality picture — source emissions and community-level receptor impact.
files_8-ambientair.applying_for_environmental_clearance_und: Applying for Environmental Clearance under EIA Notification 2006
files_8-ambientair.attr_alt_ambient_air_quality_monitoring_: Ambient air quality monitoring station deployment
Ambient air quality monitoring station outdoor environment
files_8-ambientair.can_continuous_aaqms_replace_periodic_hi: Can continuous AAQMS replace periodic Hi-Vol sampling for compliance?
files_8-ambientair.can_the_same_monitoring_data_be_used_for: Can the same monitoring data be used for both EIA and GPCB compliance?
files_8-ambientair.complete_ambient_air_quality_monitoring_: Complete ambient air quality monitoring report in CPCB/GPCB/MoEF prescribed format: all monitoring results vs. NAAQ Standards, wind roses, meteorological summaries, NABL test reports and station location maps. Ready for direct insertion into EIA chapters or EC compliance reports.
files_8-ambientair.complete_monitoring_report_in_prescribed: Complete monitoring report in prescribed format with all results, NAAQ comparison, wind roses, meteorological data and NABL certificates. Direct submission to MoEF, SEIAA or GPCB as required.
files_8-ambientair.complete_report_in_cpcbgpcb_prescribed_f: Complete report in CPCB/GPCB prescribed format: all measured concentrations tabulated against NAAQ Standards, summary statistics (mean, maximum, 98th percentile), compliance status and monitoring location maps.
files_8-ambientair.continuous_ambient_air_quality_monitorin: Continuous Ambient Air Quality Monitoring Stations (AAQMS) are increasingly accepted by CPCB and some GPCBs as an alternative or supplement to periodic Hi-Vol sampling for EC compliance reporting — particularly for PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, SO₂, NOₓ, O₃ and CO. However, they cannot replace NABL-accredited Hi-Vol analysis for all parameters specified in an EC condition, and some parameters (heavy metals, fluoride, specific VOCs) still require Hi-Vol filter collection and laboratory analysis. We advise on the most efficient combination of AAQMS continuous monitoring and targeted periodic Hi-Vol sampling to satisfy all EC conditions at minimum total cost.
files_8-ambientair.cpcb_guideline_compliant_monitoring_loca: CPCB guideline-compliant monitoring location selection considering prevailing wind direction, sensitive receptors (hospitals, schools, residential areas), topography and EC/GPCB prescribed locations. We optimise the network to provide maximum regulatory utility from minimum station count.
files_8-ambientair.cpcb_guidelines_for_eia_studies_typicall: CPCB guidelines for EIA studies typically require a minimum of 4 monitoring locations within the 5 km study area: one upwind (background), one leeward (downwind, most impacted direction), one nearest sensitive receptor (school, hospital, habitation) and one fence line location. The minimum monitoring duration is 8 consecutive 24-hour sampling days per season per location. Most EIA TOR conditions require two-season baseline (typically pre-monsoon and post-monsoon), making the total dataset 16 sampling days per location across two seasons. The number of locations can increase for larger study areas or projects with multiple emission sources — we advise on the optimal network during the scoping phase.
files_8-ambientair.developer_preparing_air_quality_chapter_: Developer preparing air quality chapter for EIA report submission
files_8-ambientair.eia_studies_require_a_seasonal_ambient_a: EIA studies require a seasonal ambient air quality baseline covering the full study area. We conduct the monitoring and prepare the air environment chapter.
files_8-ambientair.environmental_clearances_half_yearly_com: Environmental Clearances, half-yearly compliance reports, EIA studies and GPCB inspections all require valid ambient air quality data. Without it, your regulatory submissions are incomplete and rejectable.
files_8-ambientair.every_environmental_impact_assessment_st: Every Environmental Impact Assessment study requires a minimum one-season (preferably two-season) baseline ambient air quality dataset from the study area — covering a 5–10 km radius around the proposed project. This baseline is mandatory for MoEF&CC / SEIAA EC application and TOR (Terms of Reference) compliance. Without it, your EIA report is technically deficient and will be rejected at scoping.
files_8-ambientair.expansion_project_needing_post_expansion: Expansion project needing post-expansion ambient impact assessment
files_8-ambientair.fixed_or_semi_portable_automated_station: Fixed or semi-portable automated station providing real-time, continuous monitoring of PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, SO₂, NOₓ, O₃ and CO with GSM/internet data transmission. Required for EC compliance continuous monitoring conditions.
files_8-ambientair.for_ec_compliance_reporting_a_ready_to_s: For EC compliance reporting, a ready-to-submit data package covering all ambient monitoring results for the reporting period, formatted for the MoEF&CC online compliance reporting portal and SEIAA submission.
files_8-ambientair.for_eia_engagement_clients_a_fully_draft: For EIA engagement clients, a fully drafted air environment baseline chapter incorporating monitoring data, analysis, NAAQ comparison, trend discussion and predictive modelling input data — ready for inclusion in your EIA report.
files_8-ambientair.gis_referenced_site_maps_showing_all_mon: GIS-referenced site maps showing all monitoring station locations, study area boundary, sensitive receptors, wind direction overlays and land use classification — formatted for EIA chapter and regulatory submission.
files_8-ambientair.gpcb_consent_to_operate_conditions_for_r: GPCB Consent to Operate conditions for Red and Orange category industries frequently require periodic ambient air quality monitoring at specified boundary locations, submitted as part of the annual or bi-annual consent renewal package. Gaps in monitoring data are treated as consent violations during GPCB inspection, with consequences for consent renewal and category classification.
files_8-ambientair.hi_vol_sampler_and_meteorological_instru: Hi-Vol sampler and meteorological instrument installation at all designated locations. Equipment calibration verification, site security arrangements and sampling schedule commencement.
files_8-ambientair.how_many_monitoring_locations_and_sampli: How many monitoring locations and sampling days are required for an EIA baseline?
files_8-ambientair.industries_holding_environmental_clearan: Industries holding Environmental Clearances under the EIA Notification are required to submit half-yearly compliance reports to the MoEF&CC and SEIAA. These reports must include current ambient air quality monitoring data from the prescribed monitoring locations around the facility — typically the fence line, leeward side, nearest habitation and a background location.
files_8-ambientair.industry_holding_ec_with_half_yearly_com: Industry holding EC with half-yearly compliance monitoring conditions
files_8-ambientair.industry_responding_to_cpcb_gpcb_notice_: Industry responding to CPCB / GPCB notice for ambient monitoring data
files_8-ambientair.industry_with_gpcb_consent_to_operate_re: Industry with GPCB Consent to Operate requiring boundary ambient monitoring
files_8-ambientair.is_5182_methods_for_air_pollutant_measur: IS 5182 — Methods for Air Pollutant Measurement
files_8-ambientair.it_is_mandatory_for_environmental_cleara: It is mandatory for Environmental Clearance applications, EC compliance reporting, EIA studies and GPCB Consent to Operate conditions.
files_8-ambientair.low_cost_passive_diffusion_tubes_for_so_: Low-cost passive diffusion tubes for SO₂, NO₂, NH₃, H₂S and VOC monitoring over 1–4 week exposure periods. Ideal for wide-area community impact screening and supplementary spatial coverage around Hi-Vol networks.
files_8-ambientair.nabl_accredited_analysis_of_all_samples_: NABL-accredited analysis of all samples using IS 5182 methods. Internal QA/QC, outlier review and preliminary data validation before report generation.
files_8-ambientair.need_ambient_monitoring_for_your_eia_or_: Need ambient monitoring for your EIA or EC compliance?
files_8-ambientair.our_ambient_monitoring_complies_with_the: Our ambient monitoring complies with the full hierarchy of Indian environmental law and CPCB technical standards — producing data accepted by every regulatory authority, tribunal and court in India.
files_8-ambientair.project_requiring_eia_study_with_air_qua: Project requiring EIA study with air quality baseline assessment
files_8-ambientair.seasonal_wind_rose_diagrams_meteorologic: Seasonal wind rose diagrams, meteorological parameter tables (wind speed, direction, temperature, humidity, stability class) in the format required for air dispersion modelling and EIA chapter inclusion.
files_8-ambientair.signed_nabl_laboratory_analysis_reports_: Signed NABL laboratory analysis reports for all monitored parameters at all locations, accepted by MoEF&CC, SEIAA, GPCB, CPCB, courts and environmental tribunals.
files_8-ambientair.simultaneous_measurement_of_wind_speed_w: Simultaneous measurement of wind speed, wind direction, temperature, relative humidity and atmospheric stability class at each monitoring location — essential for air dispersion modelling validation and regulatory reporting.
files_8-ambientair.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_8-ambientair.standard_24_hour_filter_based_sampling_f: Standard 24-hour filter-based sampling for PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, SO₂, NO₂, CO, heavy metals and other parameters at up to 8 simultaneous locations. Portable, rapid deployment, no infrastructure required.
files_8-ambientair.study_area_mapping_wind_rose_analysis_se: Study area mapping, wind rose analysis, sensitive receptor identification, monitoring location finalisation and logistics planning per CPCB guidelines and EC/TOR conditions.
files_8-ambientair.the_ministry_of_environment_forest_and_c: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified the National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQ Standards) in 2009 under the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act. These are the legal benchmarks against which all ambient monitoring data is assessed.
files_8-ambientair.the_parameters_required_for_ec_complianc: The parameters required for EC compliance ambient monitoring are specified in your specific Environmental Clearance conditions and vary by industry category. The minimum standard set for most industries includes PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, SO₂ and NO₂. Industries with specific additional pollutant risks (e.g., cement: fluoride; chemicals: HCl, NH₃, specific VOCs; lead smelters: Pb) will have additional parameters listed in their EC. We review your EC conditions at kickoff to confirm the exact parameter list, ensure all are covered and identify any parameters requiring specialised analytical methods not available in standard Hi-Vol sampling — which require additional planning.
files_8-ambientair.typical_study_area_radius_for_eia_baseli: Typical study area radius for EIA baseline
files_8-ambientair.we_deploy_the_appropriate_monitoring_tec: We deploy the appropriate monitoring technology for each programme type — from portable Hi-Vol samplers for EIA baseline studies to online AAQMS stations for continuous real-time EC compliance monitoring.
files_8-ambientair.we_design_conduct_and_report_ambient_air: We design, conduct and report ambient air quality monitoring programmes covering EIA baseline studies, EC compliance monitoring, seasonal surveys and continuous AAQMS deployments — all producing NABL-accredited results in CPCB/GPCB-prescribed formats.
files_8-ambientair.we_provide_nabl_accredited_ambient_air_m: We provide NABL-accredited ambient air monitoring across seasonal baseline and continuous regulatory compliance programmes.
files_8-ambientair.what_parameters_are_required_for_ec_comp: What parameters are required for EC compliance ambient monitoring?
files_8-ambientair.yes_provided_the_monitoring_network_para: Yes — provided the monitoring network, parameters, sampling duration and NABL accreditation meet the requirements of both purposes. EIA baseline studies and GPCB compliance monitoring share the same NAAQ Standard benchmarks and CPCB sampling protocols. We design monitoring programmes to simultaneously satisfy EIA TOR conditions, EC compliance requirements and GPCB consent conditions wherever possible — avoiding the cost and complexity of running separate parallel programmes. The key is confirming the specific locations and parameters required by each authority before the sampling campaign begins, so that a single well-designed campaign generates regulatory-grade data acceptable to all.

files_8-cems.a_complete_cems_comprises_four_interconn: A complete CEMS comprises four interconnected subsystems that together provide legally compliant, continuous, tamper-evident emission data transmitted directly to the CPCB central server.
files_8-cems.a_six_stage_implementation_programme_fro: A six-stage implementation programme from site survey to live CPCB data transmission, typically completed in 8–14 weeks depending on site complexity and number of stacks.
files_8-cems.ambient_monitoring_around_your_facility_: Ambient monitoring around your facility boundary provides the receptor-side data that contextualises CEMS emission measurements for regulatory and community purposes.
files_8-cems.analyser_selection_qal1_certified_sampli: Analyser selection (QAL1 certified), sampling and conditioning system design, DAS specification and site layout drawing for GPCB approval.
files_8-cems.annual_parallel_reference_method_test_co: Annual parallel reference method test confirming CEMS accuracy is maintained within specified tolerance limits — required annually under EN 14181 / CPCB guidelines.
files_8-cems.attr_alt_cems_continuous_emission_monito: CEMS continuous emission monitoring system control room
files_8-cems.attr_alt_cems_installation_engineering_i: CEMS installation engineering industrial control system
files_8-cems.cement_plant_with_kiln_capacity_above_th: Cement plant with kiln capacity above threshold
files_8-cems.cems_continuous_emission_monitoring_syst: CEMS — Continuous Emission Monitoring Systems — are automated systems that measure and transmit real-time stack emission data 24×7 to the CPCB’s central data server. They are
files_8-cems.cems_installation_and_data_availability_: CEMS installation and data availability are mandatory compliance conditions in Environmental Clearances for large industries under the EIA Notification.
files_8-cems.cems_is_mandated_by_cpcb_for_17_major_in: CEMS is mandated by CPCB for 17 major industry categories under its 2014 Direction and subsequent amendments. The mandated categories include: thermal power plants, cement plants, integrated iron and steel, petroleum refineries, fertiliser plants, pulp and paper mills, sugar industries, distilleries, chlor-alkali plants, caustic soda plants, common effluent treatment plants (CETPs), common biomedical waste treatment facilities, common hazardous waste incineration and treatment facilities, glass manufacturing, tanneries, zinc and lead smelters, and copper smelters. The specific stacks, parameters and transmission requirements vary by sector. You should also check whether your GPCB or individual CTO specifies CEMS requirements beyond the national mandate, as several states have expanded the list.
files_8-cems.cems_non_compliant_or_not_yet_installed: CEMS non-compliant or not yet installed?
files_8-cems.certified_gas_analysers_measure_so_no_co: Certified gas analysers measure SO₂, NOₓ, CO, CO₂, O₂ and particulates in real time. Must be QAL1-certified under EN 14181 / CPCB specifications.
files_8-cems.cl_hcl_pm_scrubbing_stacks_and_process_v: Cl₂, HCl, PM — scrubbing stacks and process vents
files_8-cems.configuration_of_secure_gprsinternet_dat: Configuration of secure GPRS/internet data transmission to the CPCB central server in the required XML/JSON format. Connectivity testing, data validation and generation of the CPCB-required installation certificate and connectivity report.
files_8-cems.continuous_data_transmission_to_cpcb_ser: Continuous data transmission to CPCB server
files_8-cems.das_collects_timestamps_and_stores_all_a: DAS collects, timestamps and stores all analyser outputs. Calculates 15-minute averages, daily averages and emission loads. Includes tamper-evident logging.
files_8-cems.data_transmission_configuration_cpcb_ser: Data transmission configuration, CPCB server registration, connectivity test report and installation certificate preparation for regulatory submission.
files_8-cems.en_14181_qal2_calibration_report_with_sy: EN 14181 QAL2 calibration report with system calibration functions, reference method parallel test data and uncertainty analysis — required for GPCB annual submission.
files_8-cems.en_14181_qal2_calibration_using_parallel: EN 14181 QAL2 calibration using parallel reference method testing to establish system-specific calibration functions. Annual System Test (AST) with parallel reference method comparison, as required for continued CPCB compliance of the installed system.
files_8-cems.extractive_probe_inserted_into_the_stack: Extractive probe inserted into the stack draws a representative gas sample. Sample conditioning system removes moisture and particulates before analysis.
files_8-cems.fertiliser_petrochemical_plant_in_cpcb_d: Fertiliser / petrochemical plant in CPCB Direction list
files_8-cems.formal_cpcb_installation_and_connectivit: Formal CPCB installation and connectivity certificate confirming system compliance — required for submission to your GPCB as part of CTO renewal documentation.
files_8-cems.fully_operational_cems_transmitting_data: Fully operational CEMS transmitting data live to the CPCB central server — meeting all CPCB technical specifications and QAL1 analyser certification requirements.
files_8-cems.functional_cems_with_active_data_transmi: Functional CEMS with active data transmission is a mandatory condition in the CTO of all mandated industries. An inoperative or non-transmitting CEMS is treated as a violation of CTO conditions and can result in notice of consent revocation, irrespective of whether actual emissions are within limits.
files_8-cems.industry_sectors_mandated_for_cems_by_cp: Industry sectors mandated for CEMS by CPCB
files_8-cems.industry_with_cems_condition_in_consent_: Industry with CEMS condition in Consent to Operate
files_8-cems.industry_with_existing_cems_experiencing: Industry with existing CEMS experiencing data gaps or calibration failures
files_8-cems.integrated_steel_plant_with_blast_furnac: Integrated steel plant with blast furnace or sinter plant
files_8-cems.is_cems_mandatory_for_my_industry: Is CEMS mandatory for my industry?
files_8-cems.mandatory_for_17_categories_of_large_ind: mandatory for 17 categories of large industries under CPCB’s 2014 Direction and subsequent amendments.
files_8-cems.manual_isokinetic_stack_monitoring_provi: Manual isokinetic stack monitoring provides the reference method data required for QAL2 and AST calibration of your installed CEMS.
files_8-cems.maximum_data_averaging_period_for_transm: Maximum data averaging period for transmission
files_8-cems.mechanical_installation_of_probes_condit: Mechanical installation of probes, conditioning, analysers, DAS shelter. Electrical and instrument loop checks. Safety testing.
files_8-cems.minimum_data_availability_required_per_m: Minimum data availability required per month
files_8-cems.monthly_report_showing_data_availability: Monthly report showing data availability percentage, exceedance events, maintenance interventions and overall system performance against the 90% CPCB availability requirement.
files_8-cems.non_compliant_industries_face_automatic_: Non-compliant industries face automatic flagging, penalties and closure notices. We provide end-to-end CEMS services: system selection, installation, calibration, CPCB connectivity, AMC and regulatory compliance management.
files_8-cems.ongoing_scheduled_maintenance_qal2_ast_c: Ongoing scheduled maintenance, QAL2 / AST calibration, data gap management and monthly availability reports to maintain CPCB compliance.
files_8-cems.physical_installation_of_sampling_probes: Physical installation of sampling probes, conditioning systems, analysers and DAS. Site acceptance testing, zero and span calibration using certified reference gases, and initial 30-day performance demonstration before CPCB connection.
files_8-cems.pm_so_no_co_hcl_dioxinsfurans_periodic_o: PM, SO₂, NOₓ, CO, HCl, dioxins/furans (periodic), opacity
files_8-cems.pm_so_no_kiln_raw_mill_and_clinker_coole: PM, SO₂, NOₓ — kiln, raw mill and clinker cooler stacks
files_8-cems.pm_so_no_nh_prilling_towers_granulators_: PM, SO₂, NOₓ, NH₃ — prilling towers, granulators, boilers
files_8-cems.pm_so_no_sinter_plant_blast_furnace_coke: PM, SO₂, NOₓ — sinter plant, blast furnace, coke oven stacks
files_8-cems.pm_so_trs_total_reduced_sulphur_no_recov: PM, SO₂, TRS (Total Reduced Sulphur), NOₓ — recovery boiler
files_8-cems.real_time_smsemail_alerts_to_your_ehs_te: Real-time SMS/email alerts to your EHS team when CEMS data approaches or exceeds limits, allowing corrective action before CPCB automatic enforcement flags are triggered.
files_8-cems.required_for_your_facility_if_you_are: Required for your facility if you are…
files_8-cems.scheduled_preventive_maintenance_consuma: Scheduled preventive maintenance, consumables replacement (filters, probes, reference gas cylinders), corrective maintenance for analyser failures, data gap management and monthly data availability reports — maintaining minimum 90% CPCB data availability.
files_8-cems.secure_encrypted_data_transmission_to_th: Secure, encrypted data transmission to the CPCB central data server every 15 minutes. GPRS/internet connectivity with automatic retransmission on failure.
files_8-cems.selection_of_qal1_certified_analysers_an: Selection of QAL1-certified analysers and sampling systems appropriate for your stack conditions (temperature, flow, gas matrix). System design for single or multi-stack installations, including shelter, conditioning and DAS specifications.
files_8-cems.so_no_co_pm_hs_process_heaters_and_boile: SO₂, NOₓ, CO, PM, H₂S — process heaters and boilers
files_8-cems.so_no_pm_co_o_stack_gas_flow_rate_opacit: SO₂, NOₓ, PM, CO, O₂, stack gas flow rate, opacity
files_8-cems.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_8-cems.stack_characterisation_access_assessment: Stack characterisation, access assessment, gas matrix analysis and CPCB parameter list confirmation against your industry category.
files_8-cems.the_cpcb_has_mandated_cems_for_17_major_: The CPCB has mandated CEMS for 17 major polluting industry categories under its 2014 Direction. The following industries must have operational CEMS transmitting data to the CPCB server.
files_8-cems.the_cpcb_monitors_data_transmission_in_r: The CPCB monitors data transmission in real time. Gaps in data, sensor failures or CEMS downtime of more than 10% in any month automatically trigger non-compliance flags — regardless of actual emission levels.
files_8-cems.the_cpcb_server_automatically_records_da: The CPCB server automatically records data gaps. If transmission is interrupted for more than 15 minutes, the gap is logged. If total data unavailability exceeds 10% in any calendar month, the industry is automatically classified as non-compliant for that month — regardless of actual emission levels. Persistent non-compliance triggers a show-cause notice. Under our AMC, we monitor transmission health 24×7 and dispatch engineers within 4 hours of any system fault to restore connectivity and minimise data gaps. We also maintain substitute measurement data using parallel portable analysers for periods where the CEMS is unavailable for maintenance, per CPCB data substitution guidelines.
files_8-cems.the_cpcbs_central_server_monitors_data_c: The CPCB’s central server monitors data continuity in real time. Any gap exceeding 15 minutes, data transmission failure, or sensor malfunction is automatically flagged. Industries with more than 10% data unavailability in a month are listed as non-compliant — without any inspector visit.
files_8-cems.thermal_power_plant_above_25_mw_capacity: Thermal power plant above 25 MW capacity
files_8-cems.these_are_three_quality_assurance_levels: These are three quality assurance levels defined under European Standard EN 14181, which the CPCB has adopted as the reference for CEMS quality assurance in India. QAL1 is the type-approval certification of the analyser equipment itself — demonstrated by the manufacturer before sale. Only QAL1-certified analysers are accepted for CPCB-mandated CEMS. QAL2 is the site-specific calibration of a QAL1-certified analyser installed at your particular stack — conducted after installation using parallel reference method measurements. It establishes site-specific calibration functions accounting for your gas matrix. AST (Annual Surveillance Test) is a repeat parallel reference method test conducted annually to confirm that the QAL2 calibration remains valid. All three are required for a CPCB-compliant CEMS installation.
files_8-cems.we_provide_the_full_spectrum_of_cems_ser: We provide the full spectrum of CEMS services: system design, equipment supply, installation, CPCB connectivity commissioning, calibration, annual maintenance and regulatory data management.
files_8-cems.what_happens_if_our_cems_stops_transmitt: What happens if our CEMS stops transmitting data?
files_8-cems.what_is_the_difference_between_qal1_qal2: What is the difference between QAL1, QAL2 and AST in CEMS?
files_8-cems.when_cems_data_exceeds_prescribed_limits: When CEMS data exceeds prescribed limits for two or more consecutive 15-minute averaging periods, the CPCB server automatically generates an exceedance alert. This triggers regulatory correspondence and, in severe cases, immediate show-cause action without waiting for periodic manual monitoring reports.
files_8-cems.zerospan_calibration_linearity_checks_re: Zero/span calibration, linearity checks, response time testing and 30-day reliability demonstration per CPCB commissioning protocol.

files_8-stack-monitoring.a_five_stage_process_from_site_preparati: A five-stage process from site preparation to regulatory submission, delivering legally accepted results within 5–7 working days of sampling.
files_8-stack-monitoring.any_stack_subject_to_cpcbGPCB_consent_to: Any stack subject to CPCB/GPCB Consent to Operate conditions
files_8-stack-monitoring.baseline_and_compliance_ambient_air_qual: Baseline and compliance ambient air quality monitoring around your facility boundary — required for EC conditions and EIA studies.
files_8-stack-monitoring.calibration_certificates_and_records_for: Calibration certificates and records for all instruments used during sampling, required for GPCB data validation and environmental audit purposes.
files_8-stack-monitoring.chemical_process_stacks_acid_fertiliser_: Chemical process stacks — acid, fertiliser, petrochemical
files_8-stack-monitoring.coal_oil_or_gas_fired_boilers_above_thre: Coal, oil or gas-fired boilers above threshold capacity
files_8-stack-monitoring.collected_samples_are_analysed_at_our_na: Collected samples are analysed at our NABL-accredited laboratory using CPCB-prescribed analytical methods — producing results legally accepted by all GPCBs, the CPCB, courts and environmental tribunals.
files_8-stack-monitoring.complete_monitoring_report_prepared_in_s: Complete monitoring report prepared in GPCB-prescribed format with all results, calculations, equipment calibration records, field data sheets and QA/QC documentation. We prepare your submission directly for GPCB or applicable GPCB portal upload.
files_8-stack-monitoring.complete_report_in_prescribed_GPCBcpcb_f: Complete report in prescribed GPCB/CPCB format including all emission concentrations, volumetric flow rates, emission loads (kg/hr) and comparison against applicable standards with compliance status.
files_8-stack-monitoring.cpcb_general_standards_for_gaseous_emiss: CPCB General Standards for Gaseous Emissions
files_8-stack-monitoring.do_we_need_to_monitor_dg_sets_under_stac: Do we need to monitor DG sets under stack monitoring requirements?
files_8-stack-monitoring.ep_rules_1986_schedule_i_iv_standards: EP Rules 1986 — Schedule I & IV Standards
files_8-stack-monitoring.every_industry_with_a_chimney_boiler_dg_: Every industry with a chimney, boiler, DG set, kiln or furnace above the threshold capacity is legally required to conduct periodic stack emission monitoring. Non-compliance is a criminal offence under Indian environmental law.
files_8-stack-monitoring.for_repeat_clients_a_trend_analysis_comp: For repeat clients, a trend analysis comparing current results against previous monitoring cycles — identifying improving or deteriorating emissions trends before they attract regulatory attention.
files_8-stack-monitoring.full_monitoring_report_in_GPCB_format_al: Full monitoring report in GPCB format: all results vs. standards, emission load calculations, exceedance flags and corrective action recommendations.
files_8-stack-monitoring.heavy_metals_pb_hg_cd_as_cr: Heavy metals (Pb, Hg, Cd, As, Cr)
files_8-stack-monitoring.how_often_is_stack_monitoring_required: How often is stack monitoring required?
files_8-stack-monitoring.if_results_exceed_prescribed_limits_you_: If results exceed prescribed limits, you are required to submit the report as measured — falsifying results is a criminal offence. The GPCB will typically issue a show-cause notice requiring you to explain the exceedance and submit a corrective action plan with a timeline for achieving compliance. In serious or repeat cases, the GPCB can issue a closure direction. We provide an immediate written compliance advisory identifying the most probable technical causes and recommending specific corrective actions — pollution control equipment upgrades, operational changes, fuel switching or stack parameter corrections — with timeframes to achieve compliance before the next monitoring cycle.
files_8-stack-monitoring.is_5182_methods_of_measurement_of_air_po: IS 5182 — Methods of Measurement of Air Pollutants
files_8-stack-monitoring.isokinetic_particulate_sampling_in_situ_: Isokinetic particulate sampling, in-situ gas analysis, flue gas characterisation and simultaneous multi-point measurement at all stacks.
files_8-stack-monitoring.isokinetic_sampling_is_the_technique_of_: Isokinetic sampling is the technique of drawing stack gas into a sampling nozzle at exactly the same velocity as the gas flowing in the duct — so that particles enter the probe without being deflected or concentrated by velocity differences. If sampling is not isokinetic (sub- or super-isokinetic), heavier particles are either over- or under-sampled, producing results that are not representative of actual emissions. Indian Standard IS 11255 and US EPA Method 5 both mandate isokinetic sampling for particulate matter measurement. Our engineers calculate and maintain isokinetic conditions in real time throughout each sampling run.
files_8-stack-monitoring.monitoring_frequency_depends_on_your_ind: Monitoring frequency depends on your industry category and GPCB consent conditions. Red category industries are typically required to monitor quarterly; Orange category half-yearly; and Green category annually. However, your specific CTO conditions override these general timelines — some industries with high-impact stacks are required to monitor monthly. CEMS-fitted units have continuous data obligations. We review your CTO conditions at kickoff and establish the monitoring calendar to ensure you never miss a deadline.
files_8-stack-monitoring.nabl_accredited_analysis_of_collected_sa: NABL-accredited analysis of collected samples using CPCB-prescribed methods. Internal QA/QC review and result validation.
files_8-stack-monitoring.non_compliant_stack_emissions_trigger_sh: Non-compliant stack emissions trigger show-cause notices, penalties and closure orders.
files_8-stack-monitoring.on_site_inspection_of_all_emission_point: On-site inspection of all emission points, identification of stacks requiring monitoring under your CTO conditions, measurement port location review, and confirmation of sampling access arrangements before monitoring day.
files_8-stack-monitoring.on_site_real_time_measurement_of_so_no_c: On-site real-time measurement of SO₂, NOₓ, CO, CO₂ and O₂ using portable analysers calibrated against reference gases, with simultaneous measurement of flue gas temperature, velocity and moisture content per CPCB protocols.
files_8-stack-monitoring.original_signed_field_data_sheets_for_ev: Original signed field data sheets for every stack, recording sampling conditions, equipment readings, field observations and chain-of-custody documentation for all collected samples.
files_8-stack-monitoring.our_stack_monitoring_covers_the_full_hie: Our stack monitoring covers the full hierarchy of Indian environmental regulations — central, state and sector-specific — as well as international reference methods for technically complex parameters.
files_8-stack-monitoring.required_if_you_operate_any_of_these: Required if you operate any of these…
files_8-stack-monitoring.review_of_cto_conditions_identification_: Review of CTO conditions, identification of all emission points, parameter list confirmation, access and safety checks.
files_8-stack-monitoring.signed_and_stamped_test_report_from_our_: Signed and stamped test report from our NABL-accredited laboratory for all measured parameters, legally valid for regulatory submission to any GPCB, CPCB or tribunal.
files_8-stack-monitoring.GPCB_portal_submission_submission_acknow: GPCB portal submission, submission acknowledgement and post-report compliance advisory if any parameters exceed prescribed limits.
files_8-stack-monitoring.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_8-stack-monitoring.spray_dryers_rotary_dryers_and_process_h: Spray dryers, rotary dryers and process heaters
files_8-stack-monitoring.stack_emission_monitoring_is_the_legally: Stack emission monitoring is the legally mandated measurement of pollutants discharged from industrial chimneys and flue gas stacks — required by the CPCB, GPCB and MoEF&CC under the Environment Protection Act, 1986 and Water & Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Acts.
files_8-stack-monitoring.stack_emission_monitoring_sustaincore_en: Stack Emission Monitoring — SustainCore Environmental Solutions
files_8-stack-monitoring.stack_monitoring_data_forms_a_critical_i: Stack monitoring data forms a critical input to Environmental Clearance applications and half-yearly compliance reports under EC conditions.
files_8-stack-monitoring.stack_monitoring_is_mandated_under_the_e: Stack monitoring is mandated under the Environment Protection Act, 1986 and the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981. The CPCB and respective GPCBs (GPCB for Gujarat) prescribe monitoring frequency, parameters and reporting formats — typically quarterly or half-yearly for most industries.
files_8-stack-monitoring.stack_monitoring_reports_are_a_mandatory: Stack monitoring reports are a mandatory condition of every Consent to Operate issued by the GPCB. Failure to submit monitoring data on time is treated as a violation of CTO conditions and can trigger suspension or revocation of the consent — shutting operations.
files_8-stack-monitoring.the_cpcb_and_most_GPCBs_require_that_sta: The CPCB and most GPCBs require that stack emission monitoring be conducted only by NABL-accredited laboratories. Reports from non-accredited sources are rejected for regulatory submission and do not provide legal protection in show-cause proceedings or judicial hearings.
files_8-stack-monitoring.the_cpcbs_pollution_index_system_rates_i: The CPCB’s Pollution Index system rates industries Red, Orange, Green or White based on emission levels and compliance history. Consistently high stack emissions push your index score upward, attracting more frequent inspections, public reporting exposure and mandatory pollution control investment orders.
files_8-stack-monitoring.trained_field_engineers_conduct_isokinet: Trained field engineers conduct isokinetic particulate sampling and simultaneous gas phase sampling using calibrated equipment — ensuring sampling is conducted at the correct velocity to capture representative particulate matter concentrations.
files_8-stack-monitoring.we_conduct_nabl_accredited_stack_monitor: We conduct NABL-accredited stack monitoring across all industrial sectors with full regulatory report submission.
files_8-stack-monitoring.we_handle_every_stage_of_the_stack_monit: We handle every stage of the stack monitoring cycle: pre-sampling preparation, on-site isokinetic sampling and field measurement, NABL-accredited laboratory analysis, regulatory report preparation and GPCB/CPCB submission.
files_8-stack-monitoring.we_measure_all_parameters_specified_by_c: We measure all parameters specified by CPCB General Standards, GPCB / GPCB sector-specific standards and MoEF&CC Environment (Protection) Rules schedules applicable to your industry.
files_8-stack-monitoring.what_happens_if_our_stack_emissions_exce: What happens if our stack emissions exceed CPCB limits?
files_8-stack-monitoring.what_is_isokinetic_sampling_and_why_is_i: What is isokinetic sampling and why is it required for particulates?
files_8-stack-monitoring.where_cpcb_mandates_real_time_cems_we_su: Where CPCB mandates real-time CEMS, we support installation, calibration, data transmission and CPCB portal connectivity.
files_8-stack-monitoring.written_advisory_for_any_parameters_exce: Written advisory for any parameters exceeding prescribed limits, covering probable causes, immediate corrective measures and timeline recommendations to achieve compliance before the next monitoring cycle.
files_8-stack-monitoring.yes_dg_sets_above_15_kva_capacity_in_red: Yes — DG sets above 15 kVA capacity in Red and Orange category industries are subject to stack monitoring requirements and must comply with CPCB emission standards for DG sets (particulate matter, NOₓ and CO). Larger DG sets above 800 kVA may also be subject to SOₓ limits if running on high-sulphur fuel. Many companies overlook DG set monitoring, which then appears as a gap during GPCB inspections. We include all DG sets above applicable thresholds in our monitoring surveys.

files_8-workplace-air.a_comprehensive_workplace_air_quality_su: A comprehensive workplace air quality survey covers three distinct sampling zones, each answering a different question about worker exposure and emission source control.
files_8-workplace-air.a_five_stage_process_delivering_a_comple: A five-stage process delivering a complete occupational air quality assessment and corrective action plan within 10–14 working days of field sampling.
files_8-workplace-air.attr_alt_industrial_hygiene_personal_air: Industrial hygiene personal air sampling worker monitoring
files_8-workplace-air.attr_alt_workplace_air_quality_monitorin: Workplace air quality monitoring industrial hygiene sampling
files_8-workplace-air.background_and_control_zone_measurements: Background and control zone measurements confirming that engineering barriers are effective and that administrative and clerical workers near production areas are not at risk of secondary exposure.
files_8-workplace-air.chemical_processing_plant_with_solvent_a: Chemical processing plant with solvent, acid or alkali handling
files_8-workplace-air.chemical_substances_with_established_oel: Chemical substances with established OEL limits
files_8-workplace-air.chronic_occupational_diseases_silicosis_: Chronic occupational diseases — silicosis, asbestosis, chronic solvent encephalopathy, noise-induced hearing loss — attract workmen’s compensation claims, insurance litigation and reputational damage. Regular workplace monitoring, documented corrective actions and engineering controls are your primary legal defence against occupational disease compensation claims.
files_8-workplace-air.collected_samples_analysed_by_our_nabl_a: Collected samples analysed by our NABL-accredited laboratory using NIOSH, OSHA and IS 5182 analytical methods for each target analyte — producing quantified results with analytical uncertainty, detection limits and QA/QC documentation.
files_8-workplace-air.company_with_ecovadis_labour_theme_score: Company with EcoVadis Labour theme score below target
files_8-workplace-air.complete_occupational_hygiene_report_com: Complete occupational hygiene report comparing all personal and area results against applicable OELs (TWA and STEL), with exposure band classification per hazard and job group, and chronic versus acute risk assessment.
files_8-workplace-air.documentation_package_formatted_for_iso_: Documentation package formatted for ISO 45001 Clause 6.1 hazard register, Clause 8.1.1 operational control records, and Factory Inspector submission including all sampling records, field data sheets and calibration certificates.
files_8-workplace-air.documented_workplace_air_quality_monitor: Documented workplace air quality monitoring with corrective actions is one of the strongest evidence sources for improving your EcoVadis Labour & Human Rights theme score.
files_8-workplace-air.does_workplace_air_monitoring_cover_nois: Does workplace air monitoring cover noise and heat stress as well?
files_8-workplace-air.dusty_environment_cement_stone_mineral_p: Dusty environment — cement, stone, mineral processing, foundry
files_8-workplace-air.ecovadis_evaluates_occupational_health_a: EcoVadis evaluates occupational health and safety management as part of its Labour & Human Rights theme — one of the four weighted scoring criteria. Documented workplace air quality monitoring with corrective action programmes significantly improves your Labour theme score. BRSR Principle 5 (P5) requires specific KPI disclosure on workplace health and safety, including monitoring data.
files_8-workplace-air.factory_requiring_iso_45001_certificatio: Factory requiring ISO 45001 certification or recertification
files_8-workplace-air.fixed_location_samplers_across_all_produ: Fixed-location samplers across all production zones, storage areas, loading bays and control rooms — mapping the spatial distribution of contamination and identifying localised hotspots requiring priority engineering intervention.
files_8-workplace-air.fixed_samplers_placed_at_representative_: Fixed samplers placed at representative locations across the work area at breathing height (1.5m). Identifies spatial distribution of contamination and areas requiring engineering controls or improved ventilation.
files_8-workplace-air.full_nabl_report_with_exposure_assessmen: Full NABL report with exposure assessment, risk prioritisation and ranked corrective action plan covering engineering controls, administrative measures and PPE.
files_8-workplace-air.full_report_comparing_all_measured_conce: Full report comparing all measured concentrations against applicable OELs (TWA and STEL), risk-ranking each exposure scenario by severity, identifying which workers are at chronic or acute risk, and providing prioritised engineering, administrative and PPE recommendations.
files_8-workplace-air.full_shift_8_hour_personal_air_sampling_: Full-shift (8-hour) personal air sampling at the breathing zone of workers in high-exposure job categories, using calibrated low-flow pumps and appropriate sample media (XAD, OVS, silica gel, PTFE filters). Short-term samples for STEL assessment during peak-exposure tasks.
files_8-workplace-air.how_frequently_should_workplace_air_moni: How frequently should workplace air monitoring be conducted?
files_8-workplace-air.industrial_hygienist_led_facility_walk_t: Industrial hygienist-led facility walk-through to identify all chemical, dust and biological hazards, assess exposure routes, evaluate existing controls and design a targeted sampling strategy covering the most material exposure scenarios.
files_8-workplace-air.iso_45001_clause_61_requires_hazard_iden: ISO 45001 Clause 6.1 requires hazard identification and occupational health risk assessment as a core management system requirement. Workplace air quality monitoring data is the primary evidence base for chemical exposure risk assessment. Without it, an ISO 45001 audit will raise a major non-conformance.
files_8-workplace-air.it_also_directly_drives_your_ecovadis_la: It also directly drives your EcoVadis Labour score and BRSR Principle 5 disclosures on worker health and safety.
files_8-workplace-air.it_is_a_legal_duty_under_the_factories_a: It is a legal duty under the Factories Act, 1948, the Manufacture, Storage and Import of Hazardous Chemicals Rules and ISO 45001.
files_8-workplace-air.lapel_or_helmet_mounted_personal_air_sam: Lapel or helmet-mounted personal air sampler worn by the worker for the full 8-hour shift. Provides the most legally defensible measure of individual exposure. Required for ACGIH TLV/TWA comparison and chronic health risk assessment.
files_8-workplace-air.nabl_accredited_analysis_of_all_collecte: NABL-accredited analysis of all collected samples using applicable NIOSH, OSHA or IS methods with full QA/QC documentation.
files_8-workplace-air.occupational_noise_dosimetry_and_heat_st: Occupational noise dosimetry and heat stress measurement (WBGT — Wet Bulb Globe Temperature) are separate monitoring services from airborne chemical and dust monitoring, but we provide all three as part of a comprehensive industrial hygiene survey. Noise dosimetry measures workers’ daily noise exposure against the 85 dB(A) action level and 90 dB(A) limit under the Factories Act. WBGT heat stress monitoring is critical for workers in foundries, glass plants, steel mills and outdoor operations. We recommend combining air quality, noise and heat stress monitoring into a single facility survey to minimise production disruption and reduce total assessment cost.
files_8-workplace-air.oel_comparison_health_risk_classificatio: OEL comparison, health risk classification, exposure band assignment and identification of workers and zones requiring immediate or planned corrective action.
files_8-workplace-air.paint_shop_surface_treatment_or_electrop: Paint shop, surface treatment or electroplating facility
files_8-workplace-air.personal_breathing_zone_and_area_air_sam: Personal breathing zone and area air sampling across all high-risk zones and job categories. In-situ gas measurement for reactive chemicals where appropriate.
files_8-workplace-air.pesticide_or_agrochemical_production_or_: Pesticide or agrochemical production or packaging
files_8-workplace-air.ready_to_use_data_summary_for_brsr_princ: Ready-to-use data summary for BRSR Principle 5 worker health & safety KPI disclosures and EcoVadis Labour theme documentation, with key metrics formatted for questionnaire submission.
files_8-workplace-air.required_or_strongly_recommended_if_you_: Required or strongly recommended if you operate…
files_8-workplace-air.risk_prioritised_corrective_actions_cove: Risk-prioritised corrective actions covering engineering controls (LEV, enclosure, substitution), administrative measures (job rotation, exposure time limits) and PPE selection — with implementation timelines and responsible parties.
files_8-workplace-air.samplers_positioned_close_to_specific_em: Samplers positioned close to specific emission sources — open tanks, mixing operations, welding points, furnace openings — to characterise source strength for local exhaust ventilation design.
files_8-workplace-air.schedule_your_workplace_air_quality_surv: Schedule your workplace air quality survey.
files_8-workplace-air.signed_nabl_laboratory_test_report_for_a: Signed NABL laboratory test report for all monitored parameters with analytical results, detection limits, QA/QC data and chain-of-custody documentation — legally valid for Factory Inspector inspections and tribunal proceedings.
files_8-workplace-air.source_level_stack_emission_data_confirm: Source-level stack emission data confirms that control equipment is working — complementing the worker-level exposure data from indoor monitoring.
files_8-workplace-air.spatial_map_of_the_facility_showing_meas: Spatial map of the facility showing measured concentrations per zone, colour-coded by risk level (safe / action level / OEL exceeded) for visual communication to management and workers.
files_8-workplace-air.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
files_8-workplace-air.stel_reference_period_for_acute_peak_exp: STEL reference period for acute peak exposure
files_8-workplace-air.the_time_weighted_average_twa_is_the_8_h: The Time-Weighted Average (TWA) is the 8-hour average airborne concentration to which a worker may be repeatedly exposed day after day without adverse health effects — the chronic exposure limit. The Short-Term Exposure Limit (STEL) is the 15-minute average concentration that should not be exceeded at any time during the working day, even if the 8-hour TWA is within limits — it protects against acute effects from brief high-concentration exposures. Both limits are set by ACGIH as Threshold Limit Values (TLVs), and where Indian standards exist under the Factories Act Schedules, those apply. We measure both — personal full-shift sampling for TWA and short-duration grab or pump samples during peak-exposure tasks for STEL.
files_8-workplace-air.twa_reference_shift_for_chronic_exposure: TWA reference shift for chronic exposure assessment
files_8-workplace-air.under_iso_45001_and_good_industrial_hygi: Under ISO 45001 and good industrial hygiene practice, monitoring frequency depends on exposure risk level. Initial baseline monitoring should be conducted when any new chemical is introduced or processes change significantly. For ongoing surveillance: exposures below 0.1× OEL — annually or on process change; exposures 0.1–0.5× OEL — every 6 months; exposures 0.5–1× OEL — quarterly; exposures above OEL — monthly until corrective actions reduce concentrations below the limit. The Factories Act and specific industry rules (chemical, hazardous chemicals rules) may prescribe more frequent monitoring for specific substances or factory categories.
files_8-workplace-air.under_sections_1120_of_the_factories_act: Under Sections 11–20 of the Factories Act, 1948, employers have a statutory duty to maintain workplace environments free from hazardous concentrations of dust, fumes and chemicals. Factory Inspectors have powers to enter, inspect and prosecute. Failure to monitor is itself an offence, independent of whether actual exposures exceed limits.
files_8-workplace-air.unmonitored_workplace_air_quality_expose: Unmonitored workplace air quality exposes your business to criminal liability, insurance exclusions, occupational disease compensation claims, ISO 45001 non-conformances and EcoVadis score penalties — simultaneously.
files_8-workplace-air.walk_through_survey_identifying_all_chem: Walk-through survey identifying all chemical and dust hazards, existing controls assessment and sampling strategy design by a qualified industrial hygienist.
files_8-workplace-air.we_conduct_complete_industrial_hygiene_s: We conduct complete industrial hygiene surveys: hazard identification, sampling strategy design, personal and area air sampling, NABL-accredited laboratory analysis and a full occupational exposure assessment report with risk-ranked corrective recommendations.
files_8-workplace-air.we_measure_personal_and_area_concentrati: We measure personal and area concentrations against ACGIH Threshold Limit Values (TLVs), OSHA PELs and Indian Factory Schedule standards. Below are commonly monitored substances across industrial sectors.
files_8-workplace-air.what_is_the_difference_between_twa_and_s: What is the difference between TWA and STEL exposure limits?
files_8-workplace-air.with_twa_and_stel_reference_limits: with TWA and STEL reference limits
files_8-workplace-air.workplace_air_quality_monitoring_is_a_co: Workplace air quality monitoring is a core evidence requirement for ISO 45001 Clause 6.1 hazard identification and Clause 8.1 operational control compliance.
files_8-workplace-air.workplace_air_quality_monitoring_measure: Workplace air quality monitoring measures the concentration of airborne chemical, dust and biological hazards at worker breathing zones inside your facility — comparing them against Occupational Exposure Limits (OELs).
files_8-workplace-air.workplace_air_quality_monitoring_sustain: Workplace Air Quality Monitoring — SustainCore Environmental Solutions

join-us.work_across_esg_environmental_science_re: Work across ESG, environmental science, regulatory, and carbon — all under one roof

licenses-epr.a_technical_path_to_total_circularity: A technical path to total circularity.
licenses-epr.any_producer_importer_or_brand_owner_pib:
Any Producer, Importer, or Brand Owner (PIBO) that uses plastic packaging for their products is obligated
              to register on the GPCB portal and fulfill Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets through
              recycling or reprocessing.
licenses-epr.buying_credits_isnt_enough_ensuring_thos:
Buying "credits" isn't enough. Ensuring those credits are from verified, authorized
              recyclers is critical for annual compliance audits.
licenses-epr.comprehensive_fulfillment_of_extended_pr:
Comprehensive fulfillment of Extended Producer Responsibility for Plastic,
                E-Waste, Battery, and Waste Oil through GPCB portal management and verified collection.
licenses-epr.epr_regulations_are_tightening_globally_: EPR regulations are tightening globally, with heavy penalties for non-fulfillment.
licenses-epr.expert_management_of_waste_licenses_and_: Expert management of waste licenses and industrial compliance.
licenses-epr.hazardous_waste_is_categorized_into_sche:
Hazardous waste is categorized into schedules as per the Hazardous Waste Rules 2016. Authorization is
              required from the GPCB to generate, store, or dispose of such waste. We help with scientific
              characterization and ensuring disposal is only through TSDF facilities.
licenses-epr.how_is_hazardous_waste_categorized_for_a: How is hazardous waste categorized for authorization?
licenses-epr.liaising_with_state_pollution_control_bo:
Liaising with State Pollution Control Boards for CTE and CTO under Air & Water
                Acts. We ensure technical specifications meet discharge norms.
licenses-epr.navigating_complex_waste_management_and_:
Navigating complex waste management and industrial licensing. From Extended Producer
              Responsibility (EPR) to hazardous waste authorizations, we ensure your facility remains compliant and
              fully licensed.
licenses-epr.plastic_packaging_cat_i_ii_iii_iv: Plastic Packaging (Cat I, II, III, IV)
licenses-epr.securing_and_maintaining_authorisations_:
Securing and maintaining authorisations for the generation, storage, and disposal
                of hazardous and other wastes under Transboundary Movement Rules.
licenses-epr.GPCB_portals_for_epr_registration_are_of:
GPCB portals for EPR registration are often technical and require precise
              documentation that, if flawed, can lead to immediate application rejection.
licenses-epr.speak_with_our_technical_experts_to_buil: Speak with our technical experts to build your sustainability roadmap.
licenses-epr.technical_compliance_with_indias_evolvin: Technical compliance with India's evolving EPR ecosystem.
licenses-epr.the_GPCB_levies_environmental_compensati:
The GPCB levies "Environmental Compensation" (EC) based on the target shortfall. While paying EC doesn't
              absolve the PIBO of the EPR target (the target is carried over), it can result in significant financial
              impact and potential suspension of the CTO.
licenses-epr.tracking_the_amount_of_plastic_or_e_wast:
Tracking the amount of plastic or e-waste put on the market is a massive data
              challenge that requires a robust inventory management system.
licenses-epr.understanding_your_epr_and_waste_obligat: Understanding your EPR and waste obligations.
licenses-epr.we_deliver_a_full_technical_suite_for_yo:
We deliver a full technical suite for your
              EPR management.
licenses-epr.what_are_the_penalties_for_non_fulfillme: What are the penalties for non-fulfillment of EPR targets?
licenses-epr.who_needs_to_register_for_plastic_waste_: Who needs to register for Plastic Waste EPR?

management-systems.a_certificate_shouldnt_just_be_an_orname:
A certificate shouldn't just be an ornament. It should drive lower costs, fewer
              accidents, and reduced environmental impacts.
management-systems.a_system_is_only_as_good_as_the_team_run:
A system is only as good as the team running it. Without internal capacity
              building, the system collapses after the auditor leaves.
management-systems.a_technical_path_to_total_operational_ex: A technical path to total operational excellence.
management-systems.an_ims_integrates_iso_9001_quality_14001:
An IMS integrates ISO 9001 (Quality), 14001 (Environment), and 45001 (OH&S) into a single framework. This
              reduces documentation duplication, streamlines internal audits, and ensures that different management
              objectives don't conflict with each other.
management-systems.building_resilient_frameworks_for_enviro:
Building resilient frameworks for environmental and operational excellence. We
              design, implement, and audit management systems that turn compliance into a competitive advantage.
management-systems.can_we_migrate_from_ohsas_18001_to_iso_4: Can we migrate from OHSAS 18001 to ISO 45001?
management-systems.clarifying_iso_certification_and_managem: Clarifying ISO certification and management systems.

                manage environmental responsibilities in a systematic manner.

management-systems.implementing_the_iso_450012018_framework:
Implementing the ISO 45001:2018 framework to manage occupational health and
                safety risks and improve overall EHS performance.
management-systems.many_companies_have_certificates_but_lac: Many companies have certificates but lack the actual systems they represent.
management-systems.once_an_organization_is_certified_survei:
Once an organization is certified, surveillance audits are typically conducted annually to ensure
              continued adherence to the standard. A full re-certification audit occurs every 3 years. We provide
              support for NC closure and MRM preparation before every audit.
management-systems.optimizing_your_enterprise_energy_perfor:
Optimizing your enterprise energy performance through the ISO 50001 framework,
                reducing costs and greenhouse gas emissions.

management-systems.traditional_iso_systems_create_mountains:
Traditional ISO systems create mountains of paperwork that no one reads. We build
              "Lean" systems that focus on high-impact environmental metrics.

We deliver a full technical suite for your
              management system.
management-systems.what_are_the_benefits_of_an_integrated_m: What are the benefits of an Integrated Management System (IMS)?
management-systems.yes_ohsas_18001_has_been_replaced_by_the:
Yes, OHSAS 18001 has been replaced by the ISO 45001:2018 standard. We help organizations perform a gap
              analysis and migrate their existing safety management systems to the new ISO high-level structure (Annex
              SL).

              answers that generic teams often struggle to provide.
ratings-targets.creating_type_iii_environmental_labels_t:

                comparable information about the life-cycle environmental impact of products.

              improvement.
ratings-targets.double_materiality_looks_at_how_esg_issu:
Double Materiality looks at how ESG issues impact your company’s financial value (outside-in) and how your
              company impacts society and the environment (inside-out). It is now a mandatory requirement under EU CSRD
              and a best practice for high-quality disclosure.
ratings-targets.ecovadis_scores_are_relative_to_your_ind:
EcoVadis scores are relative to your industry peer group. Generally, a score above 50 earns a Bronze
              medal, while 65+ earns Silver, and 75+ earns Gold. However, global supply chains often look for continuous
              improvement year-on-year rather than just a single benchmark score.
ratings-targets.elevate_your_standing_in_global_sustaina:
Elevate your standing in global sustainability indices. We manage your performance
              across EcoVadis, CDP, and SBTi to ensure your corporate ratings reflect your true commitment to
              excellence.
ratings-targets.forest_land_and_agriculture_specific_met: Forest, Land and Agriculture specific metrics

water-testing.as_per_GPCBGPCB_norms_most_industries_ne:
As per GPCB/SPCB norms, most industries need to monitor discharge parameters daily (internal) and conduct
              NABL-certified third-party testing monthly or quarterly, depending on their "Red/Orange" category and
              consent conditions.

                Pollution Control Board (GPCB) limits.
water-testing.poor_sampling_techniques_in_the_field_le:

              multi-point collection to ensure data representativeness.
water-testing.secure_the_purity_of_your_operations_wit:

              end-to-end monitoring for Drinking Water, Effluent, and Ground Water to ensure total regulatory
              compliance.

              Mercury), and microbiological counts (E.coli). Our NABL reports provide a pass/fail summary against these
              specific limits.
